description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Dora Radić
text:
Definitely skip this place! Nothing is marked or explained, so you can’t really understand or imagine how it looked in the past. You basically stand on top, look at three rows of stacked bricks and one pool — for $35 per person, absolutely not worth it.
If you want the view, go to the nearby mountain where you get the same view for just a few euros (around €2–3).
We honestly regret wasting $70.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Anton van A
text:
Sadly another big tourist trap in Sri Lanka.. You pay 35 USD when locals pay like 99 times less than you...

Visit the next door Pidurangala instead and watch the sunrise and view of lions rock form a nice distance!

Fun fact, was at the great Pyramids of Giza earlier this year which costed less than half the price of this rock.... Avoid at all cost.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Mandy Lacy
text:
What a scam for a “world heritage site!”

We paid 36$ each to climb Sigirya which judging by the reviews fluctuates from a weekly basis. When it’s Rs120 for a local ! So as a tourist you will pay 100 more! But that happens everywhere in this country.

After I paid the expensive ticket price I popped to the loo only to have a cleaner that worked there to beg me for more money when it clearly states on the entrance to the toilet, that using the facilities are free of change.
Heading up to the top of the rock is an outrageously dangerous staircase. Once you reach the top we were completely disappointed by the change in just one year! They are completely destroying this place by covering up the original rock stairs by random bricks! Rubbish everywhere and no signs explaining anything about its history! But you will definitely find loads of signs telling you to keep off the brickwork.
The “pool” has been completely drained and no doubt will cover this up.
Totally unacceptable price and experience.
Save your money and go Polonnaruwa or take a trip to Cambodia and visit the Angkor Wat which is fantastic, rich in history

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Candice MacLennan
text:
Unfortunately it rained the day we wanted to climb, making the steps slippery and posed too much risk for us, so we chose not to climb.
What was most disappointing is the tour guides that work there and are being paid by the company were requesting 25,000LKR as a tip, the recommendation for tipping guides is $5-$10 dollars, so only 1000-2000LKR, this guy was asking 10x that, our entry ticket only cost 10,000LKR.
Please do your research before visiting these tourist attractions and falling for scams and getting ripped off.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Zainab Suratwala
text: After climbing the rock I was wondering how on earth is this place a word heritage site. The steep entrance fees is not worth the hype at all. There's absolutely nothing exotic at the top of the rock including the so called paintings. It may be a jolly climb for experienced hikers however it is extremely painful for older people or children and not advisable to be climbed. You can enjoy the view from below without paying the ridiculous entrance fees or the pictures available online. One word - avoidable.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Alexander Korovin
text: Biggest tourist trap I've ever seen. You pay 35 USD when locals pay just approx 50 cents, you climb on top, and the views are nothing special, fortress is small. Waste of time and money. Avoid this place. Better go to Piduralanga.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Grant
text:
As someone who has traveled the world to view ancient sites this was by far the most disappointing. As everything in Sri Lanka the price to enter is outrageous, but that’s to be expected as the government are in charge and you can tell due to the upkeep of the rock.

In other countries there areological sites are preserved with high quality preservation techniques, here the whole site is covered in red brick. Also there is no signs with information or depictions of the original site, so you are left scratching your head and walking round piles of newly added brickwork. Red brick is new too humans so what you see is not what it use to look like. You can see old photos of the rocks discovery and it looks nothing like it does today. The place is dirty and upkeep is bad. I have been looking forward to seeing this site for years and this is by far the most disappointing. The price is ridiculous more than Ankor wat, the great pyramids, Machu Picchu, the list goes on.

This is so so underwhelming and as a history buff I highly recommend climbing the rock opposite half the price.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Lukáš “Elvis” Vaculčík
text: Don't go there, it's a big tourist trap.  The first trap is the price of the ticket, a foreign tourist will pay 10000 rupees, for a local 120 rupees, this discriminatory approach does not belong to the civilized world.  I would expect some informational signs in the area describing the given location, unfortunately the labels are limited to the description "building x, room y".  The whole place could have a nice charm, but it's just a cash cow for the million tourists who squeeze in everywhere.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Pau Pardo
text: The entrance fee is $35, which, as a budget traveler, I could use for five days of accommodation. In my opinion, it’s not worth the price—just another way to capitalize on tourists. Instead, you can visit Pidurangala for only $3. While you won’t see the ruins, the views are just as breathtaking, with an endless green landscape stretching as far as the eye can see.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Su Ed
text:
Absolute ripoff for the overseas visitors. $35+ USD per person. hence local is only Rs.120
Not worth it unless you have a SL identity card!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Jess
text:
I completely support charging locals and foreigners different prices as culture should not be a luxury reserved for privileged people. However, I feel that the 35$ entrance ticket is not justified (specially in comparison to other attractions like to Angkor Wat at 37$, which is the biggest religious complex in the world, or the Taj Mahal at 13$).
Recommend climbing Pidurangala rock instead, 1000 LKR ticket and a beautiful sunrise and great views of the Lion’s rock and surroundings.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Wilson Favre-Delerue
text: Tourist infested landmark with shabby - if not outright dangerous - access infrastructure. One would hope for much better from a UNESCO World Heritage site. And passable views once atop.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Calin Ispravnic
text: Rip off! 35$ pp for climbing a rock  in direct sun. This price is for foreigners. Locals pay 120 LKR, less than half a dollar.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: AbdulKarim AlAradi
text:
The only rip-off in Sri-Lanka , Warning!! don’t go on weekends!! Starting payment of 30 USD for foreigners.

We went on 26th of August 2017 ,it was not a seasonal occasion however it was on weekends. A guy came to us and offered to do a tour for us for a 1,500 Rupee. He showed us that he is licensed person.

We agreed however when we went he used to walk quickly meanwhile we were slow as a tourist we want to appreciate the place, then he introduce us to people who sell a stupid book for 30 dollar that you can hide something in it but we walked away as they were pressuring us to make purchase considering it is made of “Black ebony wood” which can only be found in Sigiriya. However it is something that you will never use.

45 minutes later we reached the point where it was so crowded to get up to the lion rock as it was fully crowded and we need around 2 hours to stay in line. So he offered that we bribe the security guard on the other side an amount of 30USD and telling us that they will use the money to buy themselves a drink at night. Seriously what an excuse. And you can see the “licensed people” and the “security guards” knows each other it is just matter of benefiting each other you see them talk with each other on the sides.

After thinking about it we told him fine we will stay in the long line we will not pay... at this moment he got shocked!! he stayed for 5 minutes trying to convince us while in we were in the line after that he said I can’t continue and demanded his payment we asked him even though we know, how much is the price we agreed on “ which was 1,500” he said 4,000 after thinking for few seconds. We gave him 1,000 and asked him to get the **** out of our face. Then other “licensed tour” came to us offering that we can bribe security to pass.

Conclusion we went back to look at the beautiful place ruined by garbage employees without continuing to climb. Even big tourist buses have left as they were facing same situation, if you are a tourist you are always offered a higher price to rip you off.

I advice that you go to a village tour “20 USD” or a safari tour “50 USD”  or even elephant riding tour “1,500 Rupee” than paying “30 USD” just for half the tour in the lion rock.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

I was so surprised

To be honest

Really make shame of this place and even more make shame of Srilanka
By the way
The paint is just so so
I am sure I will never some here again

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: swathi s babu
text: Not worth the 35 dollars. Rude unhelpful staff at the front desk. Better off spending your money elsewhere

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Álex Arnal
text: Very expensive. $35 that is not invested in caring for protecting and restoring the ruins, or in the organization, or in improving the infernal stairs, or even in expelling giant wasps. Of course, the issue of capacity control doesn't even cross their minds. An hour and a half to go up and two to go down. I wouldn't go back. Go to Pirudilanga, it's better.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Eranga Sampath
text:
𝐒𝐢𝐠𝐢𝐫𝐢𝐲𝐚 𝐋𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐑𝐨𝐜𝐤  💗 👣🍃 🇱🇰

Sigiriya or Sinhagiri is an ancient rock fortress located in the northern Matale District near the town of Dambulla in the Central Province, Sri Lanka. It is a site of historical and archaeological significance that is dominated by a massive column of rock approximately 180 metres high.
.
.
Beautiful Sir Lanka 🇱🇰💚🌱

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Armando Dazza
text:
I recently visited Sigiriya, and while the site itself is historically significant, I was shocked by the ticket pricing. As a foreigner, I was charged nearly 1000 times more than what locals pay. This would be considered a scam in Europe and comes across as discriminatory. The price difference is so vast that it feels like a blatant form of racism.

As for the site, although the rock and its surroundings are impressive, much of the construction is in ruins, which makes the experience less rewarding than expected. Considering the extreme cost for foreigners, I wouldn't recommend visiting Sigiriya. There are other hills nearby with beautiful viewpoints that offer a better experience without the hefty price tag.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Matúš Drien
text:
Disappointing Experience at Sigiriya

We were really looking forward to visiting Sigiriya, but unfortunately, things went wrong from the very beginning. As soon as we parked in the official area, a man wearing what looked like an official shirt approached us. He seemed trustworthy—like a staff member of the site—and offered us a tuk-tuk tour instead of climbing the rock, claiming the hike would take two hours (which is not true). He promised to show us 10 viewpoints and attractions.

Since we were tired after a long drive from the south toward Trincomalee, we agreed to his offer—30 EUR for all three of us. Reality? We saw maybe 4 places, most of which were stops at his friends’ shops and businesses where they tried to sell us extra services. It felt like a tourist trap, with us being passed along a chain of sellers.

The tuk-tuk ride lasted barely an hour. Then he offered us a “great deal” on a safari—27,000 LKR in a Jeep. We agreed, but then he drove us outside the official site, suddenly asking for an additional 31,000 LKR for park entry—something he never mentioned before. He acted confused, ignored our questions, and became passively aggressive.

To make it worse, we received no receipt, no tickets, no confirmation—nothing official. When we insisted on a receipt, he started stalling, making excuses, and became rude. Eventually, we gave up and asked him to take us back, feeling completely scammed and frustrated.

And to top it all off, when we were leaving, a man at the parking lot demanded 500 LKR for parking. When we asked for a receipt, he quickly backed off and told us to just leave. Clearly, this was another unofficial cash grab.

⚠️ A Warning to Other Travelers:
•	The hike up Sigiriya does NOT take 2 hours. It’s much more manageable.
•	Don’t trust someone just because they wear an official-looking shirt.
•	Never pay without a ticket, receipt, or proof.
•	Always go through the official entrance and verify the information.

Sadly, this experience put a dark shadow over what should be one of Sri Lanka’s most iconic attractions. This kind of dishonest behavior is a shame for the site and damages the country’s reputation in tourism.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Max Crls
text: Crowded, expensive, Full of Slow walking people Moving up and down the stairs in slow motion. Bring enough Time! Absolutely not worth it!50$ for two is a Bad joke!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

text: Way too crowded. Takes 3h to get up and down again for 15min view (which is great though)

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya

text: Do not buy tickets online. It's useless and more expensive. You still have to get the tickets from the museum.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Chiara Bat
text:
I'm sure it's a beautiful place but unfortunately, after a long road to reach the place, I've discovered that the entrance fee (for foreigners) is 35$!!
Definitely too expensive for a budget traveller!
Not even in Rome, Greece or any other western ancient country you can expect to pay that amount of money for culture!
Cause of this absolutely crazy price I've decided not to go and save my money for some more honest and fair place.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

65 $ to see a mountain.

If you are a stranger 65 $
There is some word to descrive it?

Racism, scam and shame.
How you justify this difference of price?
This isn't the way to treat tourists.
I had to renounce to visit it

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Jitendra Patel
text:
Not worth visiting, Avoid spending money on it, Rather donate to needy and Enjoy
Price is 10000LKR
5x More then Pyramid of Giza& Sphinx
100x more then Srilankan pays

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Melissa McManus
text:
We are old & I am afraid of heights so we didn’t climb the rock. We visited the museum (AU$5 each) & then were pressured to take a 1 hour tuk tuk ride around the base with a guide. Worst decision. It was not a tour around the base but around the roads & through several villages that we had just driven with our own driver. No historical information was given & it was a complete rip off at AU$30 per person. Most beaten up tuk tuk we have been in in the 1 month we have been here. As is typical in Sri Lanka tourists are charged 50-100 times the price of locals.
The rock & Palace are amazing though.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Аntonio Fernandos
text: When I found out that the price for tourists is 100 times higher than for local residents, I refused to visit. Such discrimination is unacceptable in the modern world. In my country, the prices for tourists and local residents are the same, despite the fact that I live in a tourist city. I will take note. If I meet a Sri Lankan in my country, the price for him will be 100 times higher than for others.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Euan Anderson
text: DON'T VISIT ON A WEEKEND NOT WORTH THE MONEY. Locals get in free or don't pay enough to manage demand. We queued for 2 hours to get up and 1 hour to get down. Not worth the 35usd to see some underwhelming ruins with little sign/historical information to consume. Maybe going up at sunrise would be less of a problem but early morning is a no. Awfully run by the authorities, don't know where the money is going. The employees just stand around doing nothing not even stopping people jumping the queue.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Konstantin Vassilev
text: Shitiest experience ever. Bad organised, endless queues. Tourist rip off. You pay 30$ for endless queues with thousands of locals that get in for free and jump the queue in front of you.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Laknima Mahishani (Lucky)
text: This sigiriya is the biggest tourist scam in Sri Lanka. Its discriminate the rights of tourists and lower the quality of our rich and warm culture..the entrance fee is almost hundred times higher than locals which is 10,000 LKR for foreigners, 120 LKR for locals. Being a Sri Lankan it is a shame to see how these people around Sigiriya squeeze mone from foreigners pockets without pity. Hence I request do not waste your money on this stupid rock, it has nothing except few ruins of an old palace. I request all foreigners to not to got there and fall for this biggest tourist trap in Sri Lanka. Have some respect on your hard earn money and spend it wisely.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

The most beautiful view you can see.
Evening time visit there

However, I think to pay 36 dollars fee is a bit overpriced, I understand local have to pay  nothing, like 120 rubies but still charging tourist so much is sad. I have been travelling quite a lot and I never paid so much to visit a site. Even children have to pay 18 dollars, come on!!!!!!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Guillem Parera Gaspar
text: The place is absolutely amazing, but I had problems when trying to pay. They don’t accept € but yes $. I tried to pay with LKR and they sayed to me that must be in $.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Matthew Bott
text:
Absolute tourist scam, it is around £30 for a foreigner to go up or £0.30 for a local. I don’t believe we’d get away with charging foreigners 100 times the price in Europe, and it doesn’t usually bother me as it happens all the time throughout Asia but usually 3 or 4 times the price not 100!!

It is a much more affordable £2 to climb up Pidurangala rock which is 1km away and the views are still insane. Then I just got pictures of the fortress for free with my drone :)

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Kelsey McArthur
text: Total rip off, the rock itself is spectacular but we came from Kandy by tuk tuk which was pretty pricey, were immediately accompanied by a guide who we didn’t ask for, charged $35 USD each for entry, he rushed us through the site as quickly as possible, spent most of the time waiting in queues and when we gave him a tip at the end we were told it wasn’t enough and he normally gets $25 USD which we didn’t have on us so he proceeded to humiliate us and then drove to a nearby ATM with us to get more money out. Totally ruined the experience and I would never go back. Climb the rock next door or go to Cambodia instead.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Rocio Santervas
text: What a robbery! We pay 36USD each! We've been travelling quite a lot and it is the first time we felt ripped off! Price for tourists is 100 more expensive than locals. I get the point of paying more, but we think it is abusive and very discriminating. The place is beautiful from the bottom and views are nice at the top, but not worth it the 36usd (11065ruppes). Once more we felt robbed in Sri Lanka, if they keep doing this to tourists we won't come back or recommend it to others.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: O. Ozec
text: 36 dollar per person for tourist to climb whilst for locals it is 120 roepies (30 cents). We understand there can be a difference. But this is insane.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

I recommend using all the foreigners money you're getting to create a good experience for visitors (audio tours, water filling stations, etc) even then it would be too steep of cost. Personally these rates will probably have long term negative impacts on your tourism.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

text:
Be careful of the wasps when on site, many people went to the hospital today!
After they closed the entry and all the people who bought a ticket that could not enter, they never got a refund! 35$ per person and not able to manage the wasps, this is ridiculous!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Gary Pearson
text: Entry price of $36 is just too much, and in my case made it prohibitively expensive to go, and like the rest of Sri Lanka is just extorting money from tourists and treating them like something they have scraped off the bottom of their shoe. Money sadly is all that Sri Lanka and Sri Lankans are interested in when it comes to foreigners. Interaction almost non existent unless they are after your money. Same day in, day out, many times a day. Two tier pricing, reduced portion sizes at restaurants in comparison to locals, bus conductors giving incorrect change and driving off before you have a chance to check, general public telling a pack of lies to get free Tuk Tuk rides at hugely inflated prices, the list just goes on and on. Makes for a really miserable time for me personally and I suspect many others. Sri Lanka, this is your world heritage site for all to see and contribute to its’ upkeep, start charging accordingly. Stop trying to screw your lifeblood, the tourist, at every turn.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

text: You have to pay 30 dollars for a rock climb if you are a foreigner. It feels like a scam and discriminatory practice. There is another rock really close to sigiriya with one price for everyone and even better views.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

text: Sigiriya Is not a lion shaped rock. Lions have 5 fingers on their front legs but on Sigiriya statue has only 4 fingers. Statue has scales scares on both legs. It’s a bird shaped rock  like bird yatayu. Sigiriya belongs to king Ravana.  This fort has used by King kasiyappa for hide himself against his enemies.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Blair Waldorf
text:
It is a very nice site to visit but the people working there need so much training from different perspectives. Bad experiences happen but having your own phone  taken off your hands and being shouted at in the middle of the site when there was no clear sign of “forbidden to take photos.” I had to wait 15 mins to have my phone back. The member of staff wanted to look through all my photos. He deleted a couple of photos. Then, he decided I was clear to go but I asked him that if the problem was about those photos he should delete them in that folder as well but he replied that I could keep them in that folder for “memory”. I am still very confused about the whole issue about taking a photo then.
I am sure those people working in this UNESCO site are not interested about the stars on this review but I think it was something to share for the visitors, especially female tourists.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

text: Sigiriya is an ancient rock fortress located in the northern Matale District near the town of Dambulla in Sri Lanka. It is very beautiful and most important place for Sri Lankan people

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

name: S D
text: 22,200 Rupees. That’s how much it cost for 2 people. And at that price point, you would think the toilets would be decent. Nope. Stalls didn’t lock. No toilet paper. No hand soap. 2 out of 3 Water faucets didn’t work. Mangey monkeys and dogs with scabs everywhere. Climbing around this place is not worth the price.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Yash Paul Gupta
text: Sheer waste of time. Tickets are too costly and one cannot climb 1400 stee stairs. Nothing special to view and only mythology stories connected. Don't recommend to go

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Emma Rattray
text: I would highly recommend coming here with a guide. This is quite a spectacular site, however it lacks information and without a guide I feel you wouldn’t get the same experience. The ticket price for foreigners is very expensive 30 USD (although I heard it’s even more now). They need to realise that if you want to charge European prices (actually this is more than European prices) then you need to provide European standards. For this price people want to leave feeling educated about the site and shouldn’t have to fork out even more cash for a guide.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Joe
text: Beautiful location. Poorly managed. The Sri Lanka Tourism Promotion Bureau and Central Cultural Fund who manage this site are discriminating against residents who look foreign forcing them to pay tourist rates irrespective of presenting a national ID or visa. They also pushed for payment in USD in cash. This is a regular occurrence.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: TimTim
text: Not sure if this is right. 95 times more for foreigners?!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Malith Dilshan Peiris
text:
Actually speaking ,  for the local tourist its unfair on the starting point where you have to park the vehicle and travel 2 km straight to the entrance . If you do not like to walk only the option is to take a tuk tuk which cost Rs. 700/- .  Taking the tuk tuk is really unfair since only to the entrance from the parking area its 700 m and they have blockef by putinng some concreat slabs on the road so only the way to travel by taking the tuk tuk. .

Any how i took the tuk tuk and went and enjoy the sigiriya rock and the view. Its fine actually  but from the entrance they are not issueing the tickets . You have to walk to another place around 500m and come back to the entrance point to enter to the sigiriya. Um very dissapointed actually .

Once you done everything seeing climbing and enjoying sigiriya , if you like to take a tuk tuk as  you came you have to wait for some person to drop and take that tuk tuk and  go sonce no other tuk tuk available in that area and it will cpst you 700 for the ride back as well ,which is actually 750m driving straight to the parking lot if thoes concrete stones not put by the people. ( may be the tuk tuk department near the parking area)

Overall I am very dissapointed with my visiting to sigiriya in this time.

Its your choise to go or not to go and have the same experience as i had.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Amanda
text:
January 2024.
The entrance fee has increased from $30 to $36!!
When you're on the Lion Rock, you can't 'see' the rock itself so why even bother. Ridiculously expensive. What a tourist trap.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Shane
text: Beautiful looking place but the price to climb the rock for a tourist is ridiculous. 100X the price that the locals pay. We did not see the price worth climbing.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Senthil Kumar
text:
DON'T WASTE YOUR TIME, EFFORT AND MONEY
This is a over hyped place most probably by locals to attract tourists and extort money.
1. There is nothing special about this place, other than steep climbing of a huge rock using steel staircase.
2. Ticket for foreigners $30 per person, $15 for people from SAARC countries, for locals it is Sri Lankan Rupee 50/-.
3. The steel staircase is very narrow and most of the part climbing up and down use the same narrow path, so it slow down the movement of the people.
4. People come with big family and bring young children, so it terribly slow down the movement.  Some of the locals do not respect the Q system, they just bypass everyone and make their way in front of you with their friends and family members.
5. I went there on a full moon day (holiday in Sri Lanka), too crowded and long queue to climb up and even longer queue to climb down.
6. With so many people climbing up and down on the narrow staircase, there is a chance of stampede like situation.  NOT AT ALL SAFE.
7. I had been to many UNESCO sites, this one is a BIG disappointment.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Marlon Travel lover
text: The rock is beautiful but you have to pay 30 dollars and climb it by yourself. Paying that big amount, they should at least give a bottle of water and has a good service but there is no service at all.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Paolo Denti
text:
The place is stunning, but the price for foreigners a real robbery. 30 USD and you are probably going to spend less than half a day in there. The one star is a protest towards the extortionate prices that foreigners are charged in Sri Lanka for the main sights.
The ruins are OK, but not much is left. What makes this place amazing is the natural setting on the top of a huge monolith. A great mixture of history and nature. The frescoes in the cave are also incredibly beautiful and unlike anything I have seen before, considering they were made centuries ago, when European art was mostly inexpressive faces.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Dome Nik
text: This is a scam!!! Such discrimination!!! Locals pay 120 LKR to enter, foreigners pay 12000 LKR to enter (100 times more)!!! Do not go here, do not support such scammers. To tip it all off, there are many signs showing "co-funded by European union". Insane.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Ebsy Wooo
text: Total waste of money if you're a foreigner with the prices they charge (total rip-off). Took me 10 minutes to walk to the top using the steps. Had a stroll and looked around and got bored. Use the rock opposite (Pidurangala) as it's only 500 lkr for foreigners and far more exciting and yet provides the same views and a better experience..

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Raghavan P
text: This is the beautiful place but the service is very worst and they are charging different amounts for foreigners and local people(srilankan peoples). This the worst thing that have done by the srilankan government. This is the worst thing that I've faced in Srilanka. To be honest I'm telling the amount they charged for me, I'm the foreigner they charged nearly 5500LKR and for local people they charged 150LKR, I don't know why srilankan government doing this type of work. I wonder if this worst thing has been changed in the future. I wanted to post this review without putting a single star but I can't do this because Google can't allow to post review without putting Star. So, I'm putting a single star with disappointment.👎👎

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Lotte Leemans
text: Very disappointing experience… We arrived (2,5h trip from Kandy) and they said nothing special when we bought the tickets there.. then we entered the place and all of a sudden someone told us that you couldn’t reach the top because of a wasp problem.. they told us to wait half the way for 10-15min so the problem could go away but we waited for more than 40 min and we still couldn’t go up. Our only goal was to see the lion’s feet. We almost didn’t see anything and payed 30 dollars pp which is a lot of money over here. When we came to the cashier we told our story and they just laughed in our faces ‘no refund’ when we asked for the manager they told us he was on vacation.. we went to three different people and nobody would help us. For us it was a total rip-off. The least they could do was give a little compensation but no.. so we drove 5 hours in total for nothing..

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

Also you need to paid in dollar. It is really expensive for me for 30 usd. I have 50 dollar and the changes is 20 usd. You can can see the entrance ticket you buy is little bit dark. When i realized they give me 20usd change with little bit scratches when i finish in sigiriya. Now, i cannot change my money because of that scratches.

This place is not worth to visit. Many scam and tourist trap in this location. Even the officer doing the trap also. You must be very careful to visit this place.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Amilia Joan Fernandez
text:
DON'T WASTE YOUR TIME, EFFORT AND MONEY

This is a over hyped place most probably by locals to attract tourists and extort money. There is nothing special about this place, other than steep climbing of a huge rock using steel staircase.

Ticket for foreigners USD30 per person, USD15 for people from SAARC countries, for locals it is Sri Lankan Rupee 50/-.

The steel staircase is very narrow and most of the part climbing up and down use the same narrow path, so it slow down the movement of the people. With so many people climbing up and down on the narrow staircase, there is a chance of stampede like situation.  NOT AT ALL SAFE.

I had been to many UNESCO sites, this one is a BIG disappointment. CLIMB PIDURANGALLA ROCK INSTEAD. BETTER VIEWS, MORE FUN AND MUCH CHEAPER.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Tharanga Premadasa
text:
Poor management of Sigiriya
This incident on start of long weekend (26th September 2023) but that day hundards of school children were there for their annual trip. Long line at security entrance. Security staff very rude to viewers. We are Sri Lankan but    Security treat us like terrorists. They asked identity etc. Sigiriya is our own heritage, dont know why Security behave like that.  Management issued tickets for all people (foriegn and locals) tickets but half of them can't reach even for Sinha pada area. A lot of viewers on the day, shame on our systems lot of forigners paid around Rs.9900/= per each ticket and they had to turne back unhappily . Most of them were talking about poor facilities and poor service of management. This is what happen to our tourism.

text: Arrived at 6:30am today. Nobody was there to buy ticket. Someone arrived at 6:50am at ticket office. He said that despite having residentsy visa, I should pay full price. I was disappointed, as so far with my residential visa i have visited many places for local price. The man in the ticket office asked me to to to information desk to find out exactly the rules, but I waited until 7:20 am and nobody was there. I am merried to Sri Lankan and working in tourism industry, but this is unbelievable disappointment. The double standard and double prices are unfair! If government giving this bad example, how even the local people suppose to change? Local price is 100 rupies and foreign is 5800 ($30)!. But holding residentsy visa i would expect to be treated like local.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

name: Michael Rose
text: Beautiful rock but every 10 steps out of the couple thousand steps you get absolutely tortured. Men will grab your wife inappropriately and grope and say they are helping and literally ask for 25000lkr and you ask them not to touch your wife and call you filthy names. Tour guide at the park leads you into traps, fake tour guides lure you in and they call people on the phone and tell them your coming and set up traps to basically rob you. You tell them no and walk away and they block you in. Even in the bathroom you have to pay a man for toilet paper when you first come in. We basically got over $200 extorted from us. The park should not let these evil POS bother people coming to visit. Please avoid this place! Not worth the trip and it's $30 USD a piece and you will be harassed all the way. We didn't make it 50 steps and we left and went back to the hotel and packed and left. Hotel and Tuk Tuk driver are also part of the whole scam. Even if your use to heckling you will be cornered and have to pay to get away from these people. It ruined our day. Please please do not go here.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Carlos Scott Lopez (Ranger Golden Teacher)
text:
Extraordinary history and amazing archeology, but - as I've shared elsewhere - the policy of grossly overcharging visitors from some countries unconscionably high entry fees (rampant throughout Sri Lanka) left a horribly bitter taste. To see wealthy Sri Lankans enter for a reasonable price - and struggling students from poor, developing countries denied entry unless they paid a whopping fee - pained me immensely!

All people -- irrespective of their race, nationality, or national origin -- should be allowed entry on the same terms as everyone else...the horrific practice of crass discrimination should be stopped! Charge everyone the same price -- or charge nobody anything at all... This is the fairest approach.

And if one wants to discriminate, do so on wealth! Let the rich pay the whopping fees (including Sri Lankans!) - and those of modest means what they can afford... each person can be given the dignity of letting the attendants know which group they fall into.

For such a wonderful country in so many ways, these discriminatory entry fees are grossly detracting from an otherwise pleasant place. It's saddening and disheartening!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Andrea V
text:
It is a scam for tourists. You pay 10 times more than locals just because you are foreigners. Price has not reason to be like this.
If you don't have to mark everything your guidebook says, and follow medium tourists with "it's a must to do" because everybody says, be smart, avoid it and go to its sister, Pidurangala, one kilometer far.
Here price is 500 rupies instead 30$ (so 10 time less), view is the same if not better (you can face sigiriya), just few people, not crowdly, no vendors.
There is not sense to go here when you have something equal so close,much cheaper and maybe even better...

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Rushini
text:
If you have people with you who wouldn't join the climb,  don't bring them along in the trip to Sigiriya Rock.

The rock itself must be beautiful, but for the ones who have to sit outside and wait almost 3 hours for the others to return,  it is definitely not exciting.

I didn't join the climb because I wasn't well,  and had to wait outside as out hotel wasn't booked in Sigiriya. There were two elderly ladies sitting near the security post. The closest bathroom access was almost 800m away.  The heat of the sun was too much,  even with the shade of the tree.  I waited in the van,  but I really felt bad for the ladies.

The view was beautiful from where we were sitting.  My driver really helped by getting me snacks from the parking lot inside (which only drivers have access to pick people up)

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: florian stahl
text:
5000lkr for foreigners versus 100lkr for Sri Lanka's is not right. Go to Pidiurangala instead, it is only 500lkr, a more interesting climb and the same views.

Comparing UK average salary to Sri Lanka and looking at GDP per capita suggests UK citizens are 5-7 more wealthy than Sri Lankans. Not 50 times more wealthy.

This unfair pricing is rampant across Sri Lanka. It is incredibly off putting to tourists. Tourism supports the local businesses and brings additional income to a country. This approach makes tourists feel incredibly unwelcome.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Elena Simonetti
text:
Prezzo insensato e ingiustificato, hai quella sensazione sgradevole che per te, in quanto straniero, i soldi non abbiamo un valore. Per chi non vuole spendere 35$ a persona, consiglio di godersi la vista e l’alba da una roccia vicina che si chiama Pidurangala.
Detto questo la fortezza è molto suggestiva e la visita è di valore, semplicemente non quello che chiedono!
textTranslated:
The price is absurd and unjustified; you get that unpleasant feeling that, as a foreigner, money has no value. For those who don't want to spend $35 per person, I recommend enjoying the view and sunrise from a nearby rock called Pidurangala.
That said, the fortress is very impressive and the visit is worth it, just not what they ask for!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Lp Loft
text: Me parece excesivo el valor de u$d 35 para lo que es (ruinas) podra tener mucha historia pero realmente no tiene mucho para ver... Es un lindo lugar para conocer, morir subiendo o experimentar, pero muy caro. Y por sierto, estan restaurando todo con ladrillos nuevos y haciendo trabajos que ya hacen perder la esencia! Vayan a la otra pieda que sale 5 dolares y tienen las mismas vistas prácticamente
textTranslated: I think the $35 price tag is excessive for what it is (ruins). It might have a lot of history, but there's really not much to see... It's a beautiful place to visit, climb to your death, or experience, but it's very expensive. And by the way, they're restoring everything with new bricks and doing work that's already destroying its essence! Go to the other side of the hill, which costs $5 and has practically the same views.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Елена Лапицкая
text: Это место не стоит 35$, не организован подъем и спуск, наглые местные лезут без очереди и за взятки проводят тех, кто им заплатит. Постоять час в очереди на жаре,  чтобы только войти на саму гору, а там.... фундаменты от построек и более ничего. А потом столько же постоять назад на самой горе, чтобы спуститься....  это конечно жесть....Вид конечно на любой горе прекрасен, но... в 300 м от Сигирии есть рядом гора, так она бесплатна, народа в 10 раз меньше, виды те же. Если бы раньше такой отзыв прочитала, даже не поехала бы туда. Решать вам, стоит ли это место посещать или нет!
textTranslated: This place is not worth $35, there is no organized ascent and descent, impudent locals cut in line and take those who pay them for bribes. Stand in line for an hour in the heat just to enter the mountain itself, and there.... foundations of buildings and nothing more. And then stand the same amount of time back on the mountain itself to go down.... this is of course brutal.... The view of any mountain is of course beautiful, but... 300 m from Sigiriya there is a mountain nearby, it is free, there are 10 times fewer people, the views are the same. If I had read such a review earlier, I would not have even gone there. It is up to you to decide whether this place is worth visiting or not!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: T. Ray
text: Cette mauvaise note juste pour un coup de gueule !!!! Ça n'enlève rien à la beauté du site .... Mais payer 30$ par personne c'est du vol, les touristes ( pour ma part) ont tous un budget mis à mal par l'augmentation des billets d'avion😡. Il y a 14 ans quand je suis venu pour la première fois on était loin de ces tarifs. Bref arrêtez de nous assassiner sous prétexte qu'on soit des "touristes", j'aime ce pays mais ça devient difficile financièrement avec une famille 😞, heureusement que tout le srilanka ne pratique pas cette discrimination tarifaire !
textTranslated: This bad rating just for a rant!!!! It doesn't take away from the beauty of the site.... But paying $30 per person is theft, tourists (for my part) all have a budget put under strain by the increase in plane tickets😡. 14 years ago when I came for the first time we were far from these prices. In short, stop murdering us under the pretext that we are "tourists", I love this country but it becomes financially difficult with a family 😞, fortunately not all of Sri Lanka practices this price discrimination!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

text: Mega duur, we zijn niet op de rots gegaan omwille van de hoge inkomprijs. Er zijn mooiere plekken in de buurt die een goed zicht geven op de Lion Rock!
textTranslated: It's incredibly expensive; we didn't go up the rock because of the high entrance fee. There are nicer spots nearby that offer a good view of Lion Rock!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Nina R
text:
Prekrasno mjesto za posjetiti, ali ocjena je zbog:
ulaznice od 35 dolara - toliko se ne naplaćuju najskuplje znamenitosti svijeta!
svi novci idu vladi u džep koja minimalno ulaže u okolne pristupne ceste, u WC u muzej koji izgleda kao da je preživio apokalipsu.
Na mjestu nema pojašnjenja, samo table s nazivom što gledate i bez samostalnog istraživanja tu nećete saznati ništa o mjestu niti njegovoj povijesti. Ne dobijete niti prospekt s informacijama ili mapom sa znamenitostima što je za 36$ stvarno sramota
Domaći ljudi teško žive i jedino se nadaju turistima od kojih zarađuju, a vlada stvarno ne pomaže da se ostavi dobar dojam o zemlji
Šteta i sramota
textTranslated:
A beautiful place to visit, but the rating is due to:
$35 tickets - the world's most expensive sights don't charge that much!
all the money goes into the pocket of the government, which minimally invests in the surrounding access roads, in the toilet and in the museum that looks like it survived the apocalypse.
There is no explanation at the place, only a board with the name of what you are looking at, and without independent research you will not find out anything about the place or its history. You don't even get a prospectus with information or a map with sights, which for $36 is a real shame
The local people live hard and their only hope is tourists from whom they make money, and the government really does not help to leave a good impression of the country
Shame and shame

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

30$ pour un billet c'est juste intolérable !! Même le prix pour atteindre le sommet de la tour eiffel est moins cher !

$30 for a ticket is simply outrageous! Even the price to go to the top of the Eiffel Tower is cheaper! Sure, the view is magnificent, but I don't see why the price is so high!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Nicolas Lambert
text:
Je suis venu ici il y a deux ans, et déjà à l’époque je trouvais le prix d’entrée très élevé (25 $). Aujourd’hui, il a encore augmenté — sans explication valable — alors qu’il y a de plus en plus de touristes. On a l’impression qu’il n’y a aucune limite, et que plus les visiteurs reviennent, plus les tarifs grimpent.

Honnêtement, je ne pense plus remettre les pieds dans ce genre d’“attraction”, qui me donne de plus en plus l’impression d’un piège à touristes. Le prix est complètement disproportionné par rapport au niveau de vie du pays, et ce n’est pas seulement ici : la majorité des grands sites du Sri Lanka affichent des tarifs totalement indécents pour les étrangers.

Je comprends que le pays ait traversé des crises graves, mais faire porter ce fardeau sur les voyageurs indépendants, qui cherchent à découvrir le pays avec respect, ce n’est ni durable ni accueillant. Dommage, car le Sri Lanka est magnifique — mais à ce rythme, c’est ce genre d’expérience qui donne envie d’aller voir ailleurs.
textTranslated:
I came here two years ago, and even then, I found the entrance fee very high ($25). Today, it has increased again—without any valid explanation—even though there are more and more tourists. It feels like there's no limit, and the more visitors return, the higher the prices get.

Honestly, I don't think I'll ever set foot in this type of "attraction" again, which increasingly feels like a tourist trap. The price is completely disproportionate to the country's standard of living, and it's not just here: most of Sri Lanka's major attractions charge completely indecent prices for foreigners.

I understand that the country has gone through serious crises, but placing this burden on independent travelers, who seek to discover the country with respect, is neither sustainable nor welcoming. Too bad, because Sri Lanka is beautiful — but at this rate, it’s this kind of experience that makes you want to go elsewhere.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: N.T.M.
text: Sicherheits Garantie gibt es hier nicht,mussten lange warten. Preis Leistung ist es nicht wert .
textTranslated: There's no safety guarantee here; we had to wait a long time. It's not worth the price.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Lars Schönrock
text:
Ein unglaubliches Gemenge an Menschen. Die die hoch wollen, benutzen den gleichen Weg, wie die die runter wollen. Wenn hier Panik ausbricht, nicht auszumalen. Auf der ersten Höhe ist eine Schlange an Menschen, Wartezeit ca 1Std.
Locals zahlen 0,57€, Touristen 30$.
textTranslated:
An incredible concourse of people. Those wanting to go up use the same route as those wanting to go down. It's unimaginable what would happen if panic broke out here. There's a line of people at the first elevation, and the wait is about an hour.
Locals pay €0.57, tourists $30.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Vith S
text:
Nous avons visité Sigiriya le 23 juin 2025, en famille. Nos parents sont sri-lankais, et des documents officiels ont été présentés pour attester de notre lien de filiation.

Malgré cela, on nous a refusé le tarif local et imposé le tarif touriste, sans aucune considération pour nos attaches familiales évidentes avec le Sri Lanka.

Le tarif pour les locaux est de 120 LKR, contre 11 300 LKR pour les touristes étrangers. Soit une différence de près de 100 fois le prix, appliquée sans discernement, même lorsque l’origine familiale est clairement établie.
Je tiens à préciser que nous avons acheté les billets au tarif local (120 roupies chacun), mais l’entrée nous a été refusée, et aucun remboursement n’a été effectué.

Ce traitement soulève un vrai problème : comment justifier que les enfants de citoyens sri-lankais soient traités comme de parfaits étrangers dans un lieu public national, malgré des preuves d’origine et de lien familial claires ?

Ce n’est ni logique, ni acceptable. Cela revient à nier l’identité culturelle de toute une génération née ou vivant à l’étranger mais profondément liée au pays par ses origines.

Nous ne demandions pas un privilège, mais une reconnaissance légitime. Une politique tarifaire rigide, appliquée sans discernement, crée de l’exclusion au lieu de renforcer les liens entre la diaspora et le pays. Ce genre de décision donne le sentiment que l’on n’est pas le bienvenu, même dans le pays de ses parents.

Il serait temps que les sites culturels nationaux adoptent des critères plus justes, qui tiennent compte des réalités familiales et de l’identité. Exclure les enfants de Sri-Lankais au prétexte qu’ils vivent à l’étranger est une erreur.

En tamoule :

2025 ஜூன் 23ஆம் தேதி, எங்கள் குடும்பத்துடன் சேர்ந்து சிகிரியாவை பார்வையிட்டோம். எங்கள் பெற்றோர் இலங்கைத் தாய்த் திருமிகு குடிமக்கள், மேலும் எங்களது குடும்ப உறவை நிரூபிக்கும் அதிகாரப்பூர்வ ஆவணங்களும் சமர்ப்பிக்கப்பட்டன.

இவ்வளவு தெளிவான குடும்பத் தகவல்களும் இருக்கும்போது கூட, எங்களுக்கான உள்ளூர் கட்டணத்தை நிராகரித்து, சுற்றுலா பயணிகளுக்கான கட்டணம் திணிக்கப்பட்டது.

இலங்கை பிரஜைகள் செலுத்தும் கட்டணம் ரூ.120, ஆனால் வெளிநாட்டு சுற்றுலாப் பயணிகள் கட்டணம் ரூ.11,300. இது சுமார் 100 மடங்கு விலை வேறுபாடு, குடும்பத் தொடர் தெளிவாக உள்ளபோதிலும் எந்தவிதமான நேர்மையோ உளப்பார்வையோ இல்லாமல் விதிக்கப்பட்டது.

நாங்கள் ஒருவருக்கு ரூ.120 வீதம் உள்ளூர் கட்டணத்தில் 7 டிக்கெட்டுகள் வாங்கியிருந்தோம். இருப்பினும் எங்களை உள்ளே அனுமதிக்கவில்லை, மேலும் எங்களது பணத்திற்கான எந்தவொரு திருப்பிச் செலுத்தலும் வழங்கப்படவில்லை.

இந்த வகையான நடைமுறைகள் ஒரு மிக முக்கியமான கேள்வியை எழுப்புகிறது:
இலங்கை குடிமக்களின் பிள்ளைகள், உறுதிப்பத்திரங்களுடன் இருந்தும், எதற்காக இந்தளவுக்கு வெளிநாட்டவர்களாக கருதப்படுகிறார்கள்?

இது எந்தவிதமான தார்மீகத் தகுதியும், சமூக நியாயமும் இல்லாதது. வெளிநாட்டில் பிறந்தாலும் அல்லது வாழ்ந்தாலும், நாட்டுடன் பலமாக இணைந்திருக்கும் ஒரு தலைமுறையின் கலாச்சார அடையாளத்தையே மறுக்கிறது.

நாங்கள் சிறப்புரிமை கேட்டதில்லை – ஆனால் நியாயமான அங்கீகாரத்தை கேட்டோம்.
இவ்வாறான கடுமையான கட்டணக் கொள்கைகள், வெளிநாட்டில் உள்ள இலங்கைச் சமூகத்துடன் நாட்டுக்கிடையே உறவுகளை கட்டியெழுப்புவதற்குப் பதிலாக, விலக்குவதை உருவாக்குகின்றன. இது “நீங்கள் இங்கே சொந்தமாக இல்ல” என்பதையே உணர வைத்தது.

இப்போது நியாயமான மற்றும் அடையாள உணர்வை அடிப்படையாகக் கொண்ட நடைமுறைகளை இலங்கையின் கலாச்சார இடங்களில் அமல்படுத்த வேண்டிய நேரம் இது. வெளிநாட்டில் வாழ்கிறார்கள் என்பதற்காக இலங்கையர்களின் பிள்ளைகளை விலக்குவது ஒரு தவறு.
textTranslated:
Nous avons visité Sigiriya le 23 juin 2025, en famille. Nos parents sont Sri-Lankais, et des documents officiels ont été presento pour attester de notre lien de filiation.

Malgré cela, on nous a refusé le tarif local et impose le tarif tourist, sans aucune consideration pour nos attachés familiales evidentes avec le Sri Lanka.

Le tarif pour les locaux est de 120 LKR, contre 11 300 LKR pour les touristes étrangers. Soit une différence de près de 100 fois le prix, appliquée sans discernement, même lorsque l’origine familiale est clairement établissement.
Je tiens à précis que nous avons acheté les billets au tarif local (120 roupies each), mais l’entrée nous a été refusée, et aucun remboursement n’a été effectue.

Ce traitement soulève un vrai problème : how to justify that les enfants de citoyens Sri-lankais soient traités comme de parfaits étrangers dans un lieu public national, malgré des preuves d’origine et de lien familial claires?

Ce n'est ni logique, ni acceptable. Cela revient à nier l’identité culturelle de toute une génération née ou vivant à l’étranger mais profoundly liée au pays par ses origines.

Nous ne demandions pas un privilège, mais une recognition légitique. Une politique tarifaire rigide, applied sans discernment, crée de l’exclusion au lieu de reinforcer les links entre la diaspora et le pays. Ce genre de décision donne le sentiment que l’on n’est pas le bienvenu, même dans le pays de ses parents.

Il serait temps que les sites culturels nationaux adoptent des criteria plus justes, qui tienen compte des réalités familiales et de l’identité. Excluding Sri Lankan children on the pretext that they are living abroad is a mistake.

En tamoule :

On June 23, 2025, we visited Sigiriya with my family. Our parents are Sri Lankan citizens, and we also presented official documents proving our family relationship.

Despite such clear family information, we were refused the local fee and charged the tourist fee.

The Sri Lankan citizens pay Rs. 120, but the foreign tourists pay Rs. 11,300. This is about a 100-fold price difference, and the family connection is clear, but it was charged without any honesty or insight.

We had purchased 7 tickets at the local rate of Rs. 120 each. However, we were not allowed in, and no refund of our money was given.

These kinds of practices raise a very important question:
Why are the children of Sri Lankan citizens, even with their documents, considered foreigners?

This is devoid of any moral decency or social justice. It denies the cultural identity of a generation that is strongly connected to the country, whether born or living abroad.

We did not ask for privilege – but for fair recognition.

Such harsh tariff policies, instead of building relationships with the Sri Lankan community abroad, create exclusion. It makes us feel that “you do not belong here”.

Now is the time to implement fair and identity-based practices in Sri Lanka’s cultural spaces. It is wrong to exclude the children of Sri Lankans just because they live abroad.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Kudret Erdogan
text: 36 Dollar 33 Euro empfinde ich als eine abzocke ungefähr 11.200 LKR. Die einheimischen zahlen 100 LKR l. Das hundert fache zu verlangen von Touristen finde ich abartig und unverschämt. Egal wie arm Das Land ist.
textTranslated: I consider 36 dollars and 33 euros a rip-off, roughly 11,200 LKR. Locals pay 100 LKR per person. I find it outrageous and outrageous to charge tourists a hundred times that amount. No matter how poor the country is.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Clément Gasparini
text: Beaucoup trop cher faut pas déconner. Étant français on a des sites comme le château de Versailles qui est une excellente visite moins chère que ça... Comparons ce qui est comparable mais ne mettons pas des prix exorbitants non plus! Nous sommes déçu de voir que les Sri lankais s'intéressent plus a l'argent qu'a leur patrimoine pourtant important et intéressant. Nous ne recommandons pas, faites plutôt le caillou d'en face pour avoir la même vue certes sans les ruines mais c'est tout aussi bien!
textTranslated: Way too expensive, don't be silly. Being French, we have sites like the Palace of Versailles, which is an excellent visit, cheaper than that... Let's compare like with like, but let's not set exorbitant prices either! We are disappointed to see that Sri Lankans are more interested in money than in their heritage, which is nevertheless important and interesting. We do not recommend it, rather go to the rock opposite to have the same view, certainly without the ruins, but it's just as good!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: S. H.
text:
Viel zu teuer für das was geboten wird. Touristen zahlen aktuell $35 für mich reine Abzocke.
Der Nachbarfelsen kann ebenfalls besucht werden, da zahlt man immerhin nur $3 uns hat die gleiche Aussicht.
textTranslated:
Far too expensive for what's on offer. Tourists currently pay $35 for what I'm calling a rip-off.
The neighboring rock is also open to visitors, but it only costs $3 and offers the same view.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Grzegorz Woźniak
text: Za drogo jak za to co oferuje. 35 dolców za stanie w gigantycznej kolejce na schodach. O wiele lepiej wejść za sąsiednią górę za 2000 lkr. Widoki nieziemskie i garstka ludzi.
textTranslated: Too expensive for what it offers. 35 bucks to stand in a gigantic queue for the stairs. Much better to climb the neighboring mountain for 2,000 kr. The views are heavenly and there are only a handful of people.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: R B
text:
No vale la pena pagar 70$ por dos personas, dos horas de cola para ver una roca. Lo único que me gustó fueron los monitos que había dentro y el jardín antes de subir.
Pero es un descontrol la gente que hay.
textTranslated:
It's not worth paying $70 for two people and waiting in line for two hours to see a rock. The only things I liked were the monkeys inside and the garden before you go up.
But the crowds are uncontrolled.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Monika
text: 35$ za wstęp dla osoby spoza kraju. Cena wygórowana jak wszędzie. Naciągają nas turystów na maxa na Sri Lance. Jest tyle pięknych krajów, gdzie wstępy są bezpłatne albo w normalnych cenach. Tu nawet te pieniądze nie są inwestowane, jest bród i smród. Śmieci są wszędzie.
textTranslated: $35 entry fee for a foreigner. Exorbitant, as everywhere else. They're ripping us tourists off to the max in Sri Lanka. There are so many beautiful countries where admission is free or at regular prices. Here, even that money isn't invested; it's filthy and stinks. Garbage is everywhere.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Eva La Mer
text:
Viel zu überteuert. Abzocke! Selbst für europäische Verhältnisse total überzogener Preis. 36$ / Person. Locals 100 Rupien!!! Ich finde es unglaublich, wie Touristen in diesen Land abgezockt werden.
Die Anlage ist schön-  Wasserlöcher mit Vögel und Affen; nur noch Mauerreste übrig. Der Aufstieg ist für sportliche Personen kein Problem.
textTranslated:
Way too overpriced. A rip-off! Even by European standards, the price is completely exorbitant. $36 per person. Locals pay 100 rupees!!! I find it unbelievable how tourists are ripped off in this country.

The site is beautiful—waterholes with birds and monkeys; only remnants of walls remain. The climb is no problem for those who are reasonably fit.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Tommy
text:
16時半頃に着きました。
前回は朝来ましたがこの16時半過ぎは夕陽も見れて人が少なくおすすめです。

私はVISAがあるのでそれを見せたらまさかの結婚証明がないとダメと言われました。
何の為のVISAですか？全て揃えて提出してのVISAなのに。
結婚証明書の方が偽装が多いのに。
スリランカ政府、観光省大丈夫ですか？

娘たちと日本から来た妹だけ登りました。
ツーリストポリスでその旨を話したら今から登っていいよと言われましたがその時もう暗くなっていて登れませんでした。夜はシギリヤ入り口付近も象が出ます。

ツーリストポリスは丁寧に対応してくれました。でもこれを上に上げても上は全然対応してくれないとのこと。

折角登るのだからと日本語ガイドをつけました。8000ルピー説明してもらわないとわからないことばかりです。妹や娘たちをサポートしながら登ってくれてありがたかったです！次行く時もお願いします
textTranslated:
We arrived around 4:30 PM.
Last time I came in the morning, but after 4:30 PM, you can still see the sunset and there are fewer people, so I recommend it.

I had a visa, so when I showed it to them, they said I needed a marriage certificate.

What's a visa for? I got it after I submitted everything.

Marriage certificates are more likely to be forged.

Is the Sri Lankan government and the Ministry of Tourism OK?

Only my daughters and my sister, who came from Japan, climbed the mountain.

When I explained this to the tourist police, they said we could start climbing now, but it was already dark by then, so we couldn't go up. Elephants appear near the entrance to Sigiriya at night.

The tourist police were very polite. However, they said that even if we brought this up, they wouldn't be able to help us at all.

Since we were going all the way up, we hired a Japanese-speaking guide. There were so many things I didn't understand without an explanation, which cost 8,000 rupees. I was so grateful that they supported my sister and daughters as they climbed! I'd like to ask them again next time!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Quentin Bidault
text:
Personnellement je n’y suis pas monté. Rendez-vous compte que visiter la Tour Eiffel est moins chère !
Il faut ramener au niveau de salaire du pays, c’est vraiment énorme ! Le rocher d’en face vous coûtera 1000roupies (moins de 3€) et vous aurez une belle vue du rocher du lion.
textTranslated:
Personally, I didn't go up there. Just realize that visiting the Eiffel Tower is cheaper!
You have to consider the country's salary level; it's truly enormous! The rock opposite will cost you 1,000 rupees (less than €3), and you'll have a beautiful view of Lion Rock.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: SERGIO BARBARA
text: Il sito archeologico è molto interessante e la vista dalla rocca a 360 gradi sulla natura circostante è mozzafiato. TUTTAVIA, se siete FOREIGNERS il trattamento a voi riservato sarà decisamente diverso: partendo dal biglietto (25 dollari, nemmeno fossero i Musei Vaticani!) e continue angherie da parte del personale, che farà di tutto per farvi capire quanto gli scocci la tua presenza. Dulcis in fundo siamo stati MINACCIATI da due guardie per aver fatto una foto in un luogo “sacro” (dove da nessuna parte era segnalato il divieto) e ci hanno costretto a dare una “donazione” di cinquemila rupie altrimenti ci avrebbero portato al distretto di polizia. Roba da matti in un sito patrimonio UNESCO.
textTranslated: The archaeological site is very interesting, and the 360-degree view of the surrounding nature from the fortress is breathtaking. HOWEVER, if you're a FOREIGNER, the treatment you'll receive will be decidedly different: starting with the entrance fee ($25, like the Vatican Museums!) and constant harassment by the staff, who will do everything they can to make you understand how much they hate your presence. Last but not least, we were THREATENED by two guards for taking a photo in a "sacred" place (where there was no sign anywhere that it was forbidden) and forced to give a "donation" of five thousand rupees or they would take us to the police station. Crazy stuff in a UNESCO World Heritage site.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Giuseppe Moreschi
text: Lion's Rock, posto interessante ma non vale i soldi che costa. Il prezzo per gli stranieri é di 30 dollari, contro le 50 rupie (15 centesimi di dollaro) che pagano i locali.
textTranslated: Lion's Rock is an interesting place, but not worth the price. The price for foreigners is $30, compared to the 50 rupees (15 US cents) paid by locals.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Александр Десятов
text: Одни стукачи вокруг, получили разрешение на дрон чтоб полетать на горе лайн рок. Запустили дрон, подбежали все гиды начали говорить No Drone подбежали немцы какие то. Я говорю у меня разрешение есть, в итоге звонили сотрудниками каким то, те прибежали и сказали тоже самое, я сказал у меня разрешение есть и они ушли. Вывод:  испорченное настроение, потраченные 72 доллара за двоих, чтоб замучить ноги свои. Хочу заметить это не военный объект и не резиденция президента, а туристическое место. Лучше бы мусор чаще убирали, а не туристов с дронами гоняли.
textTranslated: Some informers around, got permission for a drone to fly on the mountain Line Rock. Launched the drone, all the guides ran up and started saying No Drone, some Germans ran up. I said I have permission, in the end they called some employees, they ran up and said the same thing, I said I have permission and they left. Conclusion: spoiled mood, spent 72 dollars for two, to torture their feet. I want to note that this is not a military facility or the presidential residence, but a tourist place. It would be better if they cleaned up the garbage more often, and not chased tourists with drones.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Javi Fariña
text: El sitio es impresionante pero no es normal que la entrada para un sitio que visitas en menos de 3 horas pagues 30 dólares por la entrada. Un local paga 100 rupis tu 9000 rupis 90 veces más... exagerado. Esto pasa en todos los lugares patrimonio de la humanidad que hay en la zona (Polonnaruwa 25 dollar) parece que no quieren que vengan turistas. Restaurantes... el triple del o cuádruple del precio normal y las raciones mucho más pequeñas... hasta que no cambien mucho las cosas este lugar no es en absoluto recomendable si quieres visitar algunos de los lugares más importantes de Sri Lanka.
textTranslated: The site is stunning, but it's not normal to pay $30 for entry to a place you can visit in less than three hours. A local pays 100 rupees, while you pay 9,000 rupees—90 times more! It's outrageous. This happens at all the World Heritage sites in the area (Polonnaruwa, $25). It seems they don't want tourists coming. Restaurants charge three or four times the normal price, and the portions are much smaller. Until things change significantly, this place is not at all recommended if you want to visit some of Sri Lanka's most important sites.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Serjio Astajof
text: Неоправданно дорогой вход и доступ к тому, чтобы самостоятельно залезть на гору и посмотреть развалины и окрестности. Цена 30$, это абсолютный зашквар!!! К примеру подъем на фуникулёре на гору Монсеррат в Боготе стоит 12$ или бесплатно пешком.
textTranslated: The entrance fee and access to climb the mountain and explore the ruins and surrounding area are unreasonably high. The price of $30 is an absolute disgrace! For example, the cable car ride up Monserrate Mountain in Bogotá costs $12, or the walk is free.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: JKK
text: Viel zu teuer und zu lange Wartezeiten. Wir sind nicht hoch. Der Preisunterschied zu den lokalen Menschen ist einfach unverschämt teuer. Lieber zum dem etwas kleineren Fels in der Nähe :)
textTranslated: Way too expensive and the wait times are too long. We're not even that high up. The price difference compared to the locals is simply outrageous. Better to go to the slightly smaller rock nearby :)

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Sam
text:
Цена не соответствует качеству услуги.
Заплатили за вход 4650 рупий (30 долларов). Для местных, не иностранцев, вход 50 рупий (30 сентов)!!! Да-да, вы не ошиблись, разница по переплате не в 2-3, а в 1000 раз!

За 30 долларов вы получаете:
- возможность посмотреть на руины высотой не выше фундамента (менее 1 метра);
- посмотреть на несколько скальных изображений 5 века н.э. под чутким взором охранника, запрещающего их фотографировать;
- унылый музей с плесенью на стенах, плохо отсканированными фотографиями и паутиной на некоторых экспонатах;
- толпы ланкийцев до самого подножия скалы, пытающихся вам что-то продать или втюхать экскурсию (всего лишь 20 долларов, мило улыбаясь, говорит вам очередной экскурсовод-самоучка=))
- полное отсутствие сервиса. Питьевая вода - только в кафе у подножия. Забыли воду и поднялись на вершину? Ваши проблемы, мучайтесь от жажды. Или платите 2000 рупий чтобы "заботливый местный ланкиец" спустился вниз и принёс вам воды с магазина.
- лавочки почти отсутствуют. Хотите посидеть - сидите на ступеньках или руинах
- ступени грубые, местами скользкие

Если бы цена билета была, например, 200 рупий - была бы твердая 5. Интересное атмосферное историческое место, стоит посетить.
Но 4650 рупий... За что мы заплатили вообще?
textTranslated:
The price doesn't match the quality of service.
We paid 4,650 rupees ($30) for admission. For locals, not foreigners, admission is 50 rupees (30 cents)!!! Yes, you read that right, the difference in overpayment isn't two or three times, but a thousandfold!

For $30, you get:
- the opportunity to view ruins no higher than the foundations (less than 1 meter);
- a look at several 5th-century AD rock carvings under the watchful eye of a guard who forbids photography;
- a dreary museum with mold on the walls, poorly scanned photographs, and cobwebs on some exhibits;
- crowds of Sri Lankans all the way to the foot of the cliff, trying to sell you something or sell you a tour (yet another self-taught tour guide tells you sweetly, "Only $20," =))
- a complete lack of service. Drinking water is only available at the cafe at the base. Forgot your water and reached the summit? That's your problem; you'll be thirsty. Or pay 2,000 rupees for a "conscientious local Sri Lankan" to come down and get you some water from the shop.
- There are almost no benches. If you want to sit, sit on the steps or ruins.
- The steps are rough, slippery in places.

If the ticket price had been, say, 200 rupees, it would have been a solid 5. An interesting, atmospheric historical site, worth a visit.
But 4,650 rupees... What did we even pay for?

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

Ca plus le prix c’était trop pour nous 🤣
textTranslated: This place might be worth seeing… but the mass tourism made us turn back. There were 50 people just at the ticket booth for the passes… That, plus the price, was too much for us 🤣

Local guide bahasa mandarin yg sangat memaksa sampai mencoba memengang.
kenyamanan dan keamanan kurang. percuma dgn cagar budaya ini.
textTranslated:
The views and history of past glories are incredible. But the corrupt uniformed staff try to trick guests into buying adult tickets for 12-year-olds. What a flawed logic. Shouldn't you pay more?
The Mandarin-speaking local guide was very pushy, even trying to hold on to you.
The comfort and security are lacking. This cultural heritage is a waste of time.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
