name: Jan Watermann
text: 30$ sind inakzeptabel. Wir befinden uns in einem Land, in dem Menschen 10$ pro Tag verdienen und damit leben können. Das wird in Sri Lanka’s Tourismus-Attraktionen leider immer sehr häufig vergessen. Der Ausländer ist hier leider immer eine wandelnde Geldbörse.
textTranslated: $30 is unacceptable. We live in a country where people earn $10 a day and can live off it. Unfortunately, this is often forgotten in Sri Lanka's tourist attractions. Foreigners here are unfortunately always a walking wallet.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Дмитрий Резниченко
text: Ехал с Канди 3 часа, чтобы успеть до 19:00, приехал в 17:10, оказывается офис продаж билетов закрывается в 17:00. На все мои уговоры и объяснения, что я хочу быть через 5 часов в Хикадуве и мне нужно максимум час времени посмотреть эту достопримечательность, отказали. Очень разочарован.
textTranslated: I drove three hours from Kandy to get there before 7:00 PM, but arrived at 5:10 PM. It turns out the ticket office closes at 5:00 PM. Despite all my persuasion and explanations that I wanted to be in Hikkaduwa in five hours and needed a maximum of an hour to see this attraction, they refused. Very disappointed.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Mermillod Alain
text:
4 heures d’attente pour arriver au sommet et personne ne nous a prévenu!
Nous étions avec notre fille de 9 ans et notre fils de 16 mois, à la billeterie personne, nous prenons donc nos tickets 70Us dollars.
Sortie de la caisse une foule incroyable, en fait il y a une 2 eme entrée qui ne servait qu’aux locaux! Et comme chaque lendemain ou jour de pleine lune les gens ne travaillent pas (nous l’avons appris après)
Nous avons demandé un remboursement au directeur qui a été abjecte, je me demande comment ai-j’en gardé mon calme.
Mauvaise expérience.
textTranslated:
A 4-hour wait to get to the top, and no one warned us!
We were with our 9-year-old daughter and 16-month-old son. No one was there at the ticket booth, so we bought our $70 tickets.
As we left the ticket booth, there was an incredible crowd. In fact, there was a second entrance that was only for locals! And since every day after or during a full moon, people don't work (we learned this later).
We asked for a refund from the manager, who was appalling. I wonder how I kept my cool.
A bad experience.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

Ne vaut pas le détour et le prix

Not worth the detour or the price.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

text: Der Eintritt kostet 30USD für Ausländer, umgerechnet fast 12’000 Rs. Ein Ticket für Einwohner kostet es 100Rs. Das heisst ich kann 120 Einwohner einladen🤦🏾‍♂️. Eine kleine Übertreibung von Sri Lanka… und ich als ehemaliger Sri Lanka Bürger wollten von mir 30USD… Abzocke!?

Les ruines n'ont que peu d'interet. En plus il y a souvent beaucoup trop de monde et on monte à la queuleuleu. A évité.
textTranslated: Everyone I met in Sri Lanka agreed that the price is far too high and that it's much better to climb Pidurangala (10 times cheaper!), which offers a beautiful view directly of Sigiriya. The ruins themselves are of little interest. Plus, it's often far too crowded, and you have to climb single file. Avoid it.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Radosław Gruszka
text:
🪜One long staircase goes up\down. If you are unlucky, you will spend most of the time on the stairs (around 2h to get to the top in my case).
⛰️The view is the only worthy thing at the top to see, except of that bricks, brick and 🧱.
💰As long as you aren't a history\architecure nerd, skip it and go to Pidurangala Rock. Oh yeah, and there's the ticket price of $35.
🤷🏻‍♂️Lion's Rock joins Anuradhapura Sacred City paid stupas for the most mediocre\skippable attractions of SL.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Paul Hawkins
text:
We were looking forward to this however we did dambulla cave temple stairs and tired from that and especially tired of the scammers, so walked to the base of the mountain and my nephew did a smaller walk to lower point to the right.

Issue are the scammers trying to get money, walked to  foreigner ticket office and do you need a tour guide NO, you should get a tour guide No, he got annoyed as we don’t want a tour guide.

Got ticket then walked to base of mountain and again do you want a tour guide NO.

Nice area old ruins.  Walked to area to right and we got do you want to buy this or that$, then do you want me to take your photo $ No, tour guide no, we have this box you want to buy  No. tuned around as pestered by people.

Then found there were shops selling the same things on way out lucky they weren’t so annoying we did buy drinks from them .

It’s meant to be a tourist place not a place for scammers to con tourists

Avoid this place until they throw the scammer out.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Sachin De Silva
text: Too crowded on weekends. Recommended to travel there early mornings. Go there if you like to see historical things otherwise you'll realize it's just a rock with some clay blocks. Sigiri graffitis are must watch!!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Arunas Pleckaitis
text:
So, I visited Sigirya Rock with my fiancee on 2025 January. I was waiting eager for this. But...Before going to SIgirya I read many reviews, gathered some info. First we where very suprised (in a bad way) then bought tickets - week ago I checked price and was said that it costs around x10 pricey for tourists than locals. Ten times its Ok, but then we bought tickets it appered that they costed one hundred (x100) times more :( for locals its 0,35 USD, for tourists 35 USD. Im my opinion its to much, you cant sqeez "money cow" so hard :D Afraid, that in the close future only locals will be climbing here... Now talk about the Rock itself - to be honest, we didnt see anything worth money for ticket, time for arriving and waiting to climb up and etc. There just couple monkeys and dogs fighting for territory :) And some brick walls. Thats all . No any info, nothing extraordinary. There is even a lot garbage, because not all tourtists (also locals) care about collecting their sh** -  bottles, papers, tissues, plastic bags, banana and etc. There is only 1 garbage bin at the exit.
So, in short - not worth the effort. Sadly, its very far from that you read and see on the internet. Its only my opinion, other people can have totally opposite opinion. Everyone is different :)
Regards dear travelers !

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Ahahahahha Ahahahha
text: So overrated, I’m aware it’s a UNESCO site but the view isn’t amazing, (better at pidurangala rock, which is a fraction of the price) you aren’t missing anything if you skip it. The entry is also very expensive if you are a backpacker and the walk isn’t as challenging as expected, very loud too with the stray dogs and right after sunset (6:30pm) they ask you to leave the rock. Best bet is to see the view point from the secret view point or pidurangala, save your money! (Also pidurangala is more peaceful imo)

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: ana castro
text:
For those with vertigo and anxiety, it's not worth the 35 USD... Hyper-touristy place with people going down and up stairs without any protection. I have vertigo and I'm fine climbing heights as long as it's not in open, crowded areas without any kind of safety.
👉UNESCO charging tourists 35 USD is very nice, but giving them security is even more so... A disgrace.
Like me, other people didn't go up.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Samuel Harder
text:
My family’s trip to Sigiriya started from the Airbnb villa we were staying at in Haragama. I made sure to have my passport, my wife had her Sri Lankan driver’s license, and we started on the 2-hour and 50-minute drive.

After arriving in Sigiriya, we parked in the foreigners’ parking lot, since I am not Sri Lankan. We separated, my wife and daughter went to the local ticketing counter, and I went to the foreign ticketing counter in the museum. Meeting back up with my wife and daughter, she said they did not charge my wife for our daughter since she was a child of 14.

We approached the area to enter Sigiriya, where there is a local ticket checking booth and a foreigner ticket checking booth. Again, we separated to gain access to the park. A man scanned my ticket, then I heard my wife ask the two ladies at the booth checking local tickets, “Why can’t my daughter go into the park? She is Sri Lankan and she is 14. I tried to pay the local price for her, the ticketing lady for locals said she is a child and it is free for Sri Lankan children”. I started walking over to the local ticketing check booth. A Sri Lankan man standing near the booth with a uniform said something to her in Sinhala. My wife looked at me and told me that our daughter is not permitted into the park and will need to go to the foreign ticketing counter to buy a ticket. I asked her what the Sri Lankan man at the ticket checking booth said. She told me he was very rude by calling her a liar, and my daughter and her need to go back. A lady from another booth came over and asked for my wife’s passport and my daughter’s passport. I looked at her with confusion. I did not realize that my wife and daughter needed a passport to be in Sri Lanka, even though both of them were born here. My wife showed her Sri Lankan ID. We had to go through emails and photos on our phones looking for my daughter’s Sri Lankan passport. After an hour in the hot sun, we were talked down to by the man and two ladies at the local ticketing booth and finally allowed entry to Sigiriya.

We learned that if you are a light-complexioned Sri Lankan, you will need a student ID or a Sri Lankan passport to eliminate discrimination, even if one or both of your parents are Sri Lankan.

My wife, daughter, and I enjoyed the exercise of climbing and the view from on top of Sigiriya. However, we decided that it is the first time and the last time we visit Sigiriya.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Manimaran M
text: It's a beautiful place for viewpoints, but otherwise, there's nothing special there. The ticketing system and prices for tourists are ten times higher than for locals, yet it could be managed better. It's a small city catering mostly to tourists, and everything is more expensive compared to Colombo." Disappointed 😥

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: D K Pow
text:
Seeing mixed reviews for the place as a “foreigner” charged USD30 / Rs10,000 (note they don’t take cards, cash only), I was a bit hesitant, but still went and I think it’s just ALRIGHT, therefore 2-star.

Of course the Pidurangala Rock climb offered more spectacular view (a bit more challenging, but doable as long as you’re careful enough) and a smaller crowd as almost all reviews suggested, I think it’s ok to see the Sigiriya Rock itself as well if you have time.

Otherwise, skip it.

My major impressions:

1. A shame that even with the charge for foreigners (200x of local fee of Rs50), the place didn’t invest much effort & money properly in maintaining the archaeological “ruins” one should expect to see at the top (many eroded), as well as the borderline-unsafe infrastructures, lack of clear signages… I’m all for supporting local economy and tourism, but did the authority utilize the foreigners’ “funding” properly?
2. People people people everywhere… (even tho I visited early at 8am!). if the charge that much for a UNESCO-listed heritage, could they at least restrict number of ppl visiting (both locals and foreigners), just like the Machu Picchu?? Like a daily or hourly cap? Seriously I don’t think the narrow metal staircase could hold that much weight with ppl on every step…
3. The primitive paper map given at the entrance gave me little info, so naturally you’d expect scammers everywhere offering to be your “guides” when you look a bit lost… the “map” was kinda a joke when you saw say a site 49, and the map had only got references for site 1-43…
4. Also a shame that I had not been told that the top part of the iconic Mirror Wall was actually CLOSED for maintenance when purchasing the ticket (I found out when chatting with a local in the summit) — and the info was also not shown anywhere online or at the site! So it’s even harder to justify the charge.

Verdict:

For this price (I know it’s hard to say whether it’s comparable coz it’s a different country), it’s equivalent to  a full-day entry price to Angkor Wat in Siem Reap (USD37) as of 2022) which’s much bigger and you can spend a whole day.

For Sigiriya Rock you’d spend just 2hrs (or at most 3 if u take more photos) for the whole climb, up and down. Also pls beware it’s already bloody hot at 9am on a sunny day, at 32 deg Celsius (April), so reasonably you wouldn’t spend much time resting on the top even in the shade unless you’ve carried plenty of ice packs with you!

Fairly disappointed to be honest.

Lower your expectations if you plan to visit.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Grasset Vivien
text:
It is a nice place even when it's cloudy as it was for me, with old history. However the ticket for foreigners is really expensive (35 USD). If you add potential scams and the cost to get there, the total cost can go very high. Be careful with guides (reject them, basically): one scammed me into telling me the price ticket including his service, only to tell me at the end I had to pay him 30 USD. I was too exhausted for a fight and alone on top of that so I accepted. But be careful.
Also it was extremely crowded the day I went.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

text: Place was beautiful but the tourist prices were bs. 120 rupees for a local vs the 10,710 rupees for a tourist is a scam, even half of that would've been better. Such a rip-off, absolutely disgusting smh.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Emine Atayurt
text:
Please save your tickets with ink stamp if you want the funds to go to conservation instead of straight into your tour guide’s pockets. You can pay with credit card. There was no line for credit card payment.

Our tour guide collected cash from most folks then told us there was an ATM but no credit card payment (which is clearly a lie). He then kept all the cash and gave people previously used tour tickets from the morning group. This enraged me that ticket fees do not go to preserve the awesome site. PLEASE hold on to your stamped ticket after ticket booth. Our tour guide collected the tickets back probably to use again with later group…

It’s a nice site but clearly poorly managed.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

No question, the view is great and the site is highly overpriced - maybe that's an approach to reduce the number of visitors the hard way?
Is it worth it? Rather not. Can be done but does not have to be done. If you are on budget climb some mountain. The real beauty reveals from below anyways.

title: Sigiriya
name: marco rossi
text: Rather overpriced for foreigners. I think it's quite fair not to charge locals and foreigners the same price, but tickets shouldn't go over 20$

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Eiba Haddad
text:
It's a beautiful rock with interesting history, however, 35 USD for foreigners is a joke. Signage can definitely be improved, so those who don't want to take a guide after paying such a crazy price can better understand and navigate the site.

For this price, I'd expect clean and modern toilets.

Also, expect slow moving crowds up and down the stairs and some groups - a certain nationality in particular- to be very loud maybe due to the lack of signage in their language telling them how to respect the site and be quiet to avoid the wasps.

Overall, it's really tiring to be treated as cash cow . Entrance fee 87 times the price for a local is A JOKE

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Nils De Clerck
text:
The views from the rock are nice, and the park around as well. However, the price seems preposterous.

Ticket price for foreigners is literally 1000 times higher than the one for locals, and you are even sent to another ticket counter. I support the fact that locals should pay less, but $36 for this rock was a lot of money for what you get. The views are just as nice from Pidurangala rock, for just 1000 rupees. Go there people!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

The hike is challenging and you get great views from the top.

However, I’m not sure if $36 is worth the entry fee. They are not doing anything to improve the “basic” facilities (for example - the toilets are disgusting!).

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

name: Kenny Darragh

Did a sunset climb and it was a great view. HOWEVER it's discrimination that "foreigners" have to pay $51aud but locals only $0.61aud. I hope the money goes towards preserving the site.

We then did the Pidurangala Rock at sunrise the next morning which was only 1000lkr with a nicer view and a more natural walk to the top.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

The place absolutely wonderful - I liked the views and hike to the top, the ancient ruins are stunning.

The big issue of this place are huge lines at the ticket booth - there are 3 sales windows but usually only one is open. We had to wait 30 minutes to buy tickets. You spend 30usd (which is a lot, I think) to enter this site, and expect the smooth purchase of the ticket, however, it is quite vice versa experience.

I asked our guide and he confirmed that it is usually like this every time.

If at least the money was also used to make sure that the area is clean etc - but unfortunately like most places lot of garbage around … we much more enjoyed the hike to the opposite mountain looking onto the lion rock (PS. Entry fee 3 USD pp)

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Sem Ekkelboom
text: Overcrowded and way too expensive. I suggest you visit the pidurangala rock instead and have the best view of the Lion Rock instead for not even 10% of the price and without pushy guides.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Bill
text: It's a very nice location and scenery. I enjoyed the view from the top, especially if you go early morning. However, I feel like they want to compensate the lost money from the crisis and the inflation with an extraordinary high price. In fact it might be one of the most expensive monuments in the world. Consider that to go up the Eifel Tower it's about 12$ and Akropolis 21$. So asking 30$ for something that is pure nature is out of place. But still not regretting of going up there.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

name: Leslie Cazaux
text: Nice site… but hugely overpriced. Especially when you know the extreme difference between local fares and foreign fares. 30$ hike and view, no guide, no bottle of water. Even Acropolis visit is cheaper. Unless you a passionate of Sri Lanka history, you can get better view for much less money. But up to you.

text: Honestly, it felt like more of a tourist trap than a historical site. The grounds are exquisite and well kept. I liked the view of the rock and the wander around the base, better than the top or the climb. Be prepared to share your experience with A LOT of people. 30 dollars is way too much.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

For the privilege of walking 1 km in the hot sun and then climbing for 2 hours, up and down. No facilities.. And we pay separately for the guide and another amount to the tuk tuks who only around the rock, fort taking photos around the place.. One is not allowed to travel in one's own vehicle.. Tourist con.. Is how I would describe it.. Sad

Do not listen or react to these cretins as they are ruining the experience for all and seriously make u feel like turning back and not even bothering. Up there and along the way are archeological sites. But not much left. The view is nice though. But still for so much money not really worth it

For having said that, the place is a must see. Unbelievable rock, where ancient building where build and paintings over 1500 years on the wall can be seen.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya

text: Too expensive, 25$, but the view from the top is awesome.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya

text: Expensive, better climb Pirangula Rock.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

text: It is difficult to climp the Rock and it is really expensive

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

text: Nothing special

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

text: This is a important place

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Swati Bharti
text: Waste of time

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Marek Wedman
text:
Świetna atrakcja lecz same wejście na góre to koszmar bardzo dużo ludzi oczywiście rozumiem że korków na schodach nie idzie uniknąć bo jest jak jest .
Ale cena za wejście dla turystów zagranicznych jest tak wysoka w porównaniu z miejscowymi że aż dyskryminująca .
Osobiście uważam że sąsiednia góra jest świetnym rozwiązaniem tańsza i nie zmarnujesz stania w korku na schodach .
Problem polega na tym że nie ma informacji dla ludzi z ograniczeniem ruchowym , starszych i rodzin z małymi dziećmi że to ciężka wspinaczka i dla tego potem stoisz w korku godzinę .
Nie polecam
textTranslated:
It's a great attraction, but the climb itself is a nightmare, with so many people. Of course, I understand that traffic jams on the stairs are unavoidable because they are what they are.

However, the entrance fee for foreign tourists is so high compared to locals that it's discriminatory.

Personally, I think the neighboring mountain is a great option; it's cheaper, and you won't waste time stuck in traffic on the stairs.

The problem is that there's no information for people with limited mobility, the elderly, and families with small children that it's a difficult climb, and that's why you end up stuck in traffic for an hour.

I don't recommend it.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Ana
text: Hemos ido a ver la Roca del León con unas expectativas demasiado grandes. Lo que nos hemos encontrado arriba fueron unas cuantas ruinas y muchos perros abandonados en muy mal estado de salud.  Las vistas seguramente son espectaculares si no llueve , pero sinceramente no merece los 35$ que hemos pagado por persona. Ha sido por desgracia una gran decepción para nosotros.
textTranslated: We went to see Lion Rock with overly high expectations. What we found at the top were a few ruins and many abandoned dogs in very poor health. The views are surely spectacular if it's not raining, but honestly, it's not worth the $35 we paid per person. It was unfortunately a huge disappointment for us.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: T.E.H.
text:
Möchte man den Felsen ohne besteigen aus nächster Nähe sehen muss man dennoch den vollen Eintritt von fast 30 US Dollar zahlen. Fanden wir jetzt nicht so schön, daher haben wir es auch gelassen.

Es gibt in der Umgebung ein paar Spots wo man den Felsen aus Entfernung sehen kann aber das ist natürlich nicht das gleiche.
textTranslated:
If you want to see the rock up close without climbing it, you still have to pay the full entrance fee of almost 30 US dollars. We didn't find that very appealing, so we decided against it.

There are a few spots in the area where you can see the rock from a distance, but of course, it's not the same.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Kamil Wójcik
text: Byliśmy i stwierdzamy że nie warto, wstęp 35 dolarów od osoby a dużo lepsze wrażenia z pidurangala rock tuż obok skąd rozciąga się widok na sigirię. Jedynie fan historii może by się tam odnalazł.
textTranslated: We've been there and we're convinced it's not worth it. Admission is $35 per person, but the Pidurangala Rock, right next door, offers a much better experience, offering views of Sigiriya. Only a history buff might enjoy it.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Maria Jose Retes
text: Sobrevalorado.La verdad es mucho más bonito desde abajo o pidurangala. Encuentro que se exceden con los precios de las entradas, es carísimo y al llegar arriba si no vas con un buen guia lo único a apreciar son las vistas.
textTranslated: Overrated. It's actually much more beautiful from below, or from Pidurangala. I think they overprice the entrance fees; it's incredibly expensive, and once you get to the top, if you don't have a good guide, the only thing you'll appreciate is the view.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Vale ZamboS
text: Mala idea haber ido un finde largo, fue una locura de gente. Tardamos en subir 2 horas... La vista es linda, las ruinas son interesantes, pero no vale 35 dólares! Me parece que se están excediendo un poco mucho con las entradas para extranjeros... No lo recomiendo. Es preferible ir a la de enfrente ver la Lion rock de lejos. Es incluso mucho más bella.
textTranslated: Bad idea to have gone for a long weekend, it was crazy crowded. It took us 2 hours to go up... The view is beautiful, the ruins are interesting, but it's not worth 35 dollars! It seems to me that they are going a little too far with the tickets for foreigners... I don't recommend it. It is preferable to go to the one opposite to see the Lion rock from afar. It's even much more beautiful.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Laure Arroyas
text: 35 dollars par adulte et 29 dollars par enfant : le prix n'est pas justifié (je compare par exemple avec les 40 dollars du Macchu Pichu ou les 12 dollars du Taj Mahal). Certes la vue est jolie, mais il ne reste pas grand chose de l'ancien palais, en tout cas pas assez pour justifier le prix !
textTranslated: $35 per adult and $29 per child: the price is not justified (I'm comparing it, for example, to the $40 for Machu Picchu or the $12 for the Taj Mahal). Admittedly, the view is nice, but there isn't much left of the old palace, certainly not enough to justify the price!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Voppe- Van drager
text: Kleine rots, 20 min nr boven. Uitzicht is opzich prima maar niks bijzonders. Te druk en zeker de prijs niet waard. Beter andere hike pakken die oprecht iets voorstelt
textTranslated: Small rock, 20 minutes to the top. The view is fine, but nothing special. Too crowded and definitely not worth the price. Better to take another hike that's actually worth it.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: 조현식
text:
가는날이 장날?지금 성수기라 현지인들 외국인들
믹스되서 주말은 피하시고 오전일찍 가시거나 오후 일몰전 시간 계산 하셔서 오르시길 추천합니다..길이 만만하지는 않으니 물챙기셔서 오르시길..시간 잘못 골라 정상까지5시간 걸렸네요
textTranslated: Is the day you go a market day? It's peak season right now, so there's a mix of locals and foreigners. I recommend avoiding weekends and going early in the morning or planning your climb before sunset. The climb isn't easy, so bring water with you. I picked the wrong time and it took me 5 hours to get to the top.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Dean Hanneken
text: Leider wurde ich heute von dem Aufstieg und der Aussicht etwas enttäuscht. Es wird alles zu touristisch angelegt und man ist trotz langer Wartezeit in ca 15 / 20 min oben. Also man wird nicht so gefordert. Es war sehr voll und naja letztlich einfach nicht so wie erwartet, wenn man den Tag davor den Felsen neben an besichtigt hat. Der Eintrittspreis ist im Vergleich zu den Einheimischen massiv hoch. 30 USD für mich zu 500/1000 Rupie für Einheimische. Pure Abzocke.
textTranslated: Unfortunately, I was somewhat disappointed by the climb and the view today. It's all too touristy, and despite a long wait, you only reach the top in about 15-20 minutes. So, it's not really a challenge. It was very crowded, and well, ultimately, it just wasn't what I expected, especially after visiting the rock formation next door the day before. The entrance fee is massively high compared to what locals pay. 30 USD for me versus 500-1000 rupees for locals. Pure rip-off.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Таролог-Астролог Ксения Новикова
text: Место не стоит 36$. Не интересное место. Груда камней.
textTranslated: The place isn't worth $36. It's not an interesting place. It's just a pile of rocks.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: melda ersoy
text: Eğer 1500 merdiven çıkarım diyosanız gidin tabii ama 1500 merdivenin sonunda göreceğiniz manzara ne kadar mutlu eder tartışılır, ben çok beğenmedim
textTranslated: If you want to climb 1500 stairs, go for it, but it's debatable how happy you'll be with the view you'll see at the end of 1500 stairs. I didn't like it very much.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Ayoze
text: El lugar es bonito pero nos pareció demasiado caro para lo que ofrece, 35$. Lo pasamos mucho mejor subiendo la otra montaña gratuita y sacando fotos de esta
textTranslated: The place is nice but we found it too expensive for what it offers, $35. We had a much better time climbing the other free mountain and taking pictures of this one.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Tomasz Konstanty Wojciechowski
text: Niebrzydkie miejsce, na pewno nie za te pieniądze. Gdybym przyjechał tu jeszcze raz to zrobiłbym zdjęcie z dołu i pojechał dalej. Na górze nie ma nic poza wejściem z łapami lwa i jaskinią z kilkoma malowidłami, których nie można fotografować... Widoki przeciętne, na pewno nie warte 36 USD
textTranslated: Not a bad place, definitely not worth the price. If I were to visit again, I'd take a photo from the bottom and move on. At the top, there's nothing but an entrance with lion paws and a cave with a few paintings that aren't allowed in photos... The views are average, definitely not worth the $36.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Felix R
text:
Schöner Ort, aber mit 33 € für Ausländer dann doch ziemlich happig.
Außerdem ist meistens sehr viel los. Lieber den Nachbarfelsen (3€) nehmen und die schöne Aussicht genießen.
textTranslated:
It's a beautiful place, but at €33 for foreigners, it's quite steep.

Plus, it's usually very busy. It's better to climb the neighboring rock (€3) and enjoy the beautiful view.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Amke
text:
Eigentlich ein sehr schöner Ort, der uns gut gefallen hat. Die Natur ist herrlich und oben hat man einen guten Ausblick. Ruinen sind auch interessant.
ABER: Der Ort ist das Geld nicht wert, 30 $ Eintritt pro Person, dafür ist die Anlage vergleichsweise schlecht in Schuss. Das Museum... Naja. Cafeteria im Inneren des komplexes war geschlossen (und das nicht erst seit gestern). Schilder? Kaum vorhanden.  Alles im allen sein Geld nicht wert...
Direkt nebenan ist ein Felsen auf den man für 5 $ pro Person steigen kann und von dort hat man auch eine schöne Aussicht!
textTranslated:
It's actually a very beautiful place that we really enjoyed. The scenery is gorgeous, and the view from the top is great. The ruins are interesting too.

BUT: The place isn't worth the money. $30 entrance fee per person, and the site is in relatively poor condition. The museum... well, let's just say it's not great. The cafeteria inside the complex was closed (and had been for some time). Signage? Hardly any. All in all, not worth the money... Right next door, there's a rock you can climb for $5 per person, and the view from there is also beautiful!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Alexia Enet
text:
Le coût est exorbitant pour les étrangers (36 dollars). La vue est belle et le site superbe. Mais pas à ce prix. Le Pidurangala en face est à 1000 rps et a une vue bien plus belle.
Fait le matin, pas trop chaud pr la montée et pas trop de monde.
textTranslated: The cost is exorbitant for foreigners ($36). The view is beautiful and the site superb. But not at that price. Pidurangala across the way costs 1000 rupees and has a much better view. I went in the morning; it wasn't too hot for the climb and there weren't too many people.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Sergei Fen’kiv
text: Смотреть объективно нечего… Тем более если были в подобных местах по миру. Если только очень нечего смотреть езжайте. Полистайте фото в интернете и этого будет достаточно.
textTranslated: There's nothing objectively to see... Especially if you've been to similar places around the world. Only go if there's really nothing to see. Browse photos online and that will be enough.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Philippe & Louisa
text: Très décevant, très cher pour ce que c’est. Alors oui la vue est belle mais les jardins ne sont pas exceptionnels et ce qu’il reste du complexe historique est loin de valoir les 30$ que l’on vous demande. Si c’était à refaire et bien… on ne le ferait pas ! Le temple de Dambulla ou le site de Polonnaruwa sont bien plus intéressants à tout point de vue.
textTranslated: Very disappointing, very expensive for what it is. So yes the view is beautiful but the gardens are not exceptional and what remains of the historic complex is far from worth the $30 that is asked of you. If we had to do it again, well... we wouldn't! The temple of Dambulla or the site of Polonnaruwa are much more interesting from every point of view.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Max Hůlka
text: Nebyl jsem nahoře ale jenom dole kvůli fotce. Jeden pán nám nabízel jízdu tuktukem za 50$ odmítly jsme a pán furt otravoval
textTranslated: I wasn't at the top, I was just at the bottom for a photo. A man offered us a tuk-tuk ride for $50, we refused and the man kept pestering us.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Данил Жарков
text: Здесь кажется уже писали, но лучше подняться на соседнюю гору и полюбоваться Сигирией со стороны. Точно не стоит потраченных 35$.
textTranslated: I think it's been mentioned here before, but it's better to climb the neighboring mountain and admire Sigiriya from a distance. It's definitely not worth the $35.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Dominik Jordi
text: Die Preise sind unglaublich hoch und der Unterschied, welche Touristen im Vergleich zu Einheimischen zahlen müssen darf in dem Ausmass nicht sein. Einheimische zahlen 120 LKR und Touristen 11257 LKR (36$) als Eintritt. Das ist das 93-Fache😱 einfach nur unverschämt und schade.
textTranslated: The prices are incredibly high, and the difference between tourists and locals shouldn't be this great. Locals pay 120 LKR and tourists pay 11,257 LKR ($36) for entry. That's 93 times the price. It's simply outrageous and a shame.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Arnaud Schlesser
text: GROSSE déception. Le site est beau oui, mais il manque des explications, un peu d’entretien et surtout, le prix est inadmissible ! Environ 60 fois plus cher que pour les Sri Lankais ! 30 euros pour monter c’est beaucoup trop, mais quand on sait que c’est 50 centimes pour les habitants du Sri Lanka, c’est pire. Les touristes ne sont pas tous riches, les touristes ne reviendront pas au Sri Lanka si les gens continuent de les arnaquer c’est logique…!!
textTranslated: BIG disappointment. The site is beautiful yes, but it lacks explanations, a little maintenance and above all, the price is unacceptable! About 60 times more expensive than for Sri Lankans! 30 euros to go up is way too much, but when you know that it's 50 cents for the inhabitants of Sri Lanka, it's worse. Tourists are not all rich, tourists will not come back to Sri Lanka if people continue to rip them off, it's logical...!!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: J
text: Schöner Berg, sehr hoher Preis
textTranslated: Beautiful mountain, very high price

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Grégory Leulier
text:
Dix fois trop cher pour monter sur le rocher.
Faites le tour, c'est gratuit et il y'a une ambiance sympa
textTranslated:
Ten times too expensive to climb the rock.
Take the tour; it's free and there's a nice atmosphere.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Tiphanie HYS
text:
Incroyable et absolument unique en son genre. Il y a de quoi en prendre pleins les yeux !
Bémol à mes yeux, pour les voyageurs en sac à dos et petits budgets, le ticket d'entrée en devient presque un fossé dans le budget quotidien. Très dommage par rapport à la symbolique de ce lieu, et au reste du pays...
textTranslated:
Incredible and absolutely unique. It's a feast for the eyes!
The downside, in my opinion, is that for backpackers and travelers on a budget, the entrance fee is almost a dent in their daily budget. A real shame, considering the symbolism of this place, and the rest of the country...

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Алёна Семёнова
text: Сразу скажу, моё мнение субъективно. Но. Все, что есть хорошего на Сигирии это виды с неё.  Ценник на билеты неоправданно завышен. Из исторического лапки льва, 5 фресок и куча кирпичей. Может быть подойдёт тем, кто не доехал в Полоннаруву
textTranslated: Let me say right away that my opinion is subjective. But the only good thing about Sigiriya is the view. The ticket prices are unreasonably high. The historical part is a lion's paw, five frescoes, and a bunch of bricks. Maybe it's suitable for those who didn't make it to Polonnaruwa.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Иван
text: Дорогой входной билет, красиво на верху, но не соразмерно потраченным деньгам, времени и силам. В галерею наскальных рисунков пожалел, что поднялся.
textTranslated: Expensive entrance fee, beautiful at the top, but not commensurate with the money, time and effort spent. I regretted going up to the rock painting gallery.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Blechi Blechmen
text: Ja, das stimmt, Touristen werden abgezockt. aber nicht nur hier. Wir hatten zum Glück einen einheimischen Führer, der uns die ganze Gegend zeigte. Von den sogenannten "Himmelsmädchen" (die Felsmalereien beim Aufstieg an der Wand ) war ich allerdings enttäuscht. Von den wurde so geschwärmt..
textTranslated: Yes, it's true, tourists get ripped off, but not only here. Luckily, we had a local guide who showed us the whole area. I was disappointed, however, by the so-called "Sky Girls" (the rock paintings on the wall as we climbed). Everyone raved about them.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Corina B.
text:
der lion rock an sich ist toll und die aussicht wunderbar!
der eintritt von USD 30 pro person ist aber abriss. die einheimischen bezahlen USD 0.30! ausserdem ist das museum sehr in die jahre gekommen und die räume sehr finster.
es empfiehlt sich sehr früh (7:00 Uhr) vor dem grossen andrang dort zu sein. wir waren um 8:00 uhr dort und gingen in einer kolonne den berg hoch.
textTranslated:
Lion Rock itself is great, and the view is wonderful!

The entrance fee of USD 30 per person is ridiculous, though. Locals pay USD 0.30! Furthermore, the museum is very dated, and the rooms are very dark.

It's advisable to get there very early (7:00 a.m.) before the crowds arrive. We were there at 8:00 a.m. and walked up the mountain in a convoy.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Win
text: Skała nawet ok ale nie rozumiem kompletnie po co piąć się tyle w górę i płacić krocie by zobaczyć z góry drzewa?Wiem,że to miejsce historyczne,nie jestem ignorantem,wszedłem tam na górę i zadałem sobie pytanie.Po co?Dla mnie zobaczyć  z zewnątrz i spadać.
textTranslated: The rock is okay, but I don't understand at all why climb so high and pay a fortune to see the trees from above? I know it's a historic site, I'm not ignorant, I climbed up there and asked myself a question. Why? For me, I could see from the outside and then fall.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Eva Medina Yoga
text: Demasiado  valorado. Pagas 27 euros para subir y ver unas pezuñas  de leon y cuateo ruinas. Consigues alcanzar la cima, haciendo procesion en fila, asfixiados de calor, y la baja más  de lo mismo. Casi dos horaa de tortura rodeada de locales. No repetiria teniendo su vecina Pirindungala entrente pagando 3,5 euros y con esas maravillosas vistas.
textTranslated: Overrated. You pay 27 euros to go up and see lion hooves and four ruins. You manage to reach the top, marching in a single file, suffocating from the heat, and the descent is more of the same. Almost two hours of torture surrounded by locals. I wouldn't go back, considering its neighboring Pirindungala is just 3.5 euros and has such wonderful views.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Clemens Brosch
text: Interessante Archäologische Stätte. Die vielen Besucher und der ausländische Eintrittspreis der um das 125 fache höher!!! ist als der für Einheimische trüben das Gesamtbild.
textTranslated: An interesting archaeological site. However, the large number of visitors and the entrance fee for foreigners, which is 125 times higher than for locals, detract from the overall impression.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Ch. H.
text: Man muß schon zeitig dort sein, ansonsten sehr viele Menschen.  Nervig sind die Führer und Tultuk Fahrer. Sie kleben einem an der Ferse. Irgendwie will jeder nur Geld.
textTranslated: You have to get there early, otherwise it's very crowded. The guides and Tultuk drivers are annoying. They're always on your heels. Somehow, everyone just wants money.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Valentin Lastique
text: Très beau park et un beau point de vue, mais comme d'habitude au sri lanka les touristes payent cher, trop cher... 30 dollars pour monter sur un rocher, c'est un peu du vol...
textTranslated: A very beautiful park and a lovely viewpoint, but as usual in Sri Lanka, tourists pay a lot, too much... 30 dollars to climb onto a rock is a bit of a rip-off...

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: dave maud
text: Très joli site, un peu trop bondé et un peu attrape touristes à plus 5300 rps ! On peut lui préférer Pidurangala qui offre une belle vue sur le rocher au lion. Attention aux accompagnateurs qui proposent de vous aider, ils ont des polos logotypés association de défense de Sigirya ou équivalent et ils vous demanderons des sommes importantes, sous le regard des guides et gardien... une mafia !
textTranslated: A very pretty site, but a bit too crowded and a bit of a tourist trap at over 5300 rupees! Pidurangala is a better choice, offering a beautiful view of Lion Rock. Beware of guides offering to help you; they wear polo shirts with the logo of the Sigiriya Defense Association or something similar, and they will ask for large sums of money, all under the watchful eyes of the guides and guards... it's like a mafia!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Сергей «Sawaoff» Балмасов
text: Неоправданно дорого, вид сверху не впечатляет, от слова совсем. Самым увлекательным оказались подъем и обезьяна, утащившая последние два глотка имбирного пива.
textTranslated: Unreasonably expensive, the view from the top is not impressive at all. The most exciting part was the climb and the monkey that stole the last two sips of ginger beer.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: David Beraldo
text: Troppa gente, prezzo troppo alto, ovviamente il sito è molto interessante ma non so se ne vale la pena a questa cifra.
textTranslated: Too many people, too high a price. Obviously the site is very interesting, but I don't know if it's worth it at this price.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

name: kyung sung Kim
text: 크게 볼 것도 없는 곳에 입장 요금만 비싸고 편의 시설이 전무하다.
textTranslated: There is nothing much to see, the entrance fee is expensive, and there are no amenities.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

Zu voll,
Cooler Felsen

Too crowded,
Cool rock

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya

text: Entradas desorbitadas a 30 dolares. Otros viajeros prefieren ver Pidurangala como alternativa.
textTranslated: Tickets are exorbitant at $30. Other travelers prefer to see Pidurangala as an alternative.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Franck HAUSER
text: Beaucoup trop cher pour une simple vue… aller sur l’autre rocher la vue y est beaucoup plus agréable
textTranslated: Far too expensive for just a view… go to the other rock, the view is much nicer there.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: 曉羽丁
text: 去上個廁所被收2000rs，原本說20，到真的要收錢直接*100倍，太壞了。
textTranslated: I was charged 2000 rupees just to use the restroom. They originally said it would be 20, but when they actually tried to charge me, they multiplied it by 100. That's outrageous.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Quentin Brabant
text: Je trouve que le prix de l'entrée est dérisoire, 1500 roupees par personne pour aller voir une 50 de bouddha quasi tous identique
textTranslated: I find the entrance fee ridiculously low, 1500 rupees per person to see 50 Buddhas, almost all identical.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Cecilia Rojas López
text: Caro, abarrotado de gente, se puede ver solo rapidamente las pinturas despues de hacer horas una fila. Prefiero Pidurungula de todas maneras
textTranslated: Expensive, overcrowded, and you can only see the paintings briefly after waiting in line for hours. I prefer Pidurungula anyway.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Johann S (Johann S)
text:
OK. Sympa, mais totalement hors de prix pour ce que c'est, et ultra blindé de gens...

Personnellement, j'ai vraiment préféré Pidurangala Rajamaha Viharaya
textTranslated:
Okay. Nice, but totally overpriced for what it is, and packed with people...

Personally, I really preferred Pidurangala Rajamaha Viharaya.

text: This place is overhyped in my opinion. If you are someone who doesn't enjoy trekking much then you can avoid it. The view after reaching the rock is good though but it's a difficult journey, you literally have to climb a lot of stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Habib
text: It's more about beautiful rock and wonderful scenery than word heritage site, and nothing to do with heritage. The climb to the top takes around 35-45 minutes. It’s a bit of a hard workout, but worth it if you love panoramic view of green SriLanka. It's too difficult if you don't have good fitness or you have disability. Entry costs 35$.
