text: Nice area, walk up and view. However the price is not worth it. Its' robbery and tourist trap managed by government.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Go Ceylon
text:
Historical Marvel:
Sigiriya, or Lion Rock, is a testament to ancient Sri Lankan ingenuity, with its impressive fortress and intricate water gardens.
Its UNESCO World Heritage status is well-deserved.
The Climb:
The ascent involves navigating a series of steps and pathways, which can be challenging, especially in the heat.
The climb can generally take between 1.5 to 3 hours for the ascent and descent.
Factors that affect the time:
Fitness level
Crowd density
Time spent at each viewing point
Key pathway features:
Steps of varying heights and widths.
Spiral staircases leading to the frescoes.
The iconic Lion's Paws staircase.
It is important to note that some parts of the walk, especially the spiral staircases, can be daunting for those with vertigo.
Key Attractions:
The Sigiriya frescoes are a must-see, showcasing ancient artistry.
The Mirror Wall offers a glimpse into the past with its ancient graffiti.
The summit provides breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding landscape.
The water gardens at the base of the rock are also very impressive.
Tips for Visitors:
Wear comfortable walking shoes.
Bring plenty of water to stay hydrated.
Visit early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the midday heat.
Be prepared for crowds, especially during peak season.
Consider the time of year you are visiting, because during the hotter months, it is very important to avoid the mid day sun.
Overall:
Sigiriya is a must-visit destination for anyone traveling to Sri Lanka.
The climb is rewarding, offering stunning views and a glimpse into the island's rich history.
Be prepared for a physical challenge.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: marzieh mohammadpour
text: With 1200 steps, it is worth seeing once, not more. It is similar to Alamut Castle in Qazvin, Iran, although with a different story.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Molly R
text:
We visited here in August and although it was beautiful views, the experience wasn’t great: it took us approximately 2.5 hours of constant slow queuing (alongside 1000s of people) to get up and down the rock … which for us just wasn’t worth it, they also charged tourists $35 per ticket to get in … instead I would recommend going to the rock opposite (Pidurangala rock) if you want more of a climb experience with a lot less people and much lower cost

To climb the rock the stairs are very steep and elderly/children were struggling - so that’s worth noting

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Rasiah Yogalingam
text: A historical place.with a destroyed king's palace above a rock at a height.steps are used to climb the rock takes atleast one hour.At the top of the rock.Destroyed palace, lakes and built, rock steps can be seen. Paintings in a cave also can be seen.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Manjula Medagoda
text:
Climbing the Sigiriya is extraordinary and had such wonderful experience. The day I visited Sigiriya Rock with my family and I was charged entrance fee unfairly.
I was charged local price but my wife and daughter was charged tourists price.
Even though we are holding enough evidence.
Again tickets counter and Administration are not in standard position and lack of customer service politeness doesn’t help to improve our tourism

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Lukas Krupa
text:
Fine,
1) The view is one and only experience you might not have anywhere in Sri Lanka , ancient ruins of former fortress had a but chill and respect to it.
2) The gardens are awesome to observe , plus paintings are well kept secret you might want to see.
3) UNESCO site - if you want to cross of some check marks from your list.
but …
1) 36$ seems to be a bit much if you consider locals pay ~100x less - I dont see the money spent on the premises (considering this is main tourist attraction).
I would imagine the money we spent here could get us a lot more anywhere else - huge disappointment.

2) Local guides are rude if you dont pick one.

Heads up/notes:
1) Climbing should be done early in the morning or later evening to avoid heat attacks.
2) Usual hikers should have no problem climbing up - its about 30 min up , people who do not hike - there are rest points but it may be harder - take care and prepare a bit.
3) no need for whole boots , but flipflops arent really best pick :)

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Shanks
text:
Visited recently, overall mixed experience. Entry tickets for Indian-SAARC nationals were discounted, so do identify yourself at the counter, we paid 5960 LKR each (₹1730 approx.) which still felt very overpriced for this excursion at the end.

The hike takes 30-60 minutes, depending on fitness, with stairs and frequent rest stops. At the Lion’s Paw midway, there’s a rest area with a water refill station—a gift from India that made us feel both happy and proud. It’s a great touch that encourages reducing plastic waste, so recommended to bring your own bottle.

We reached the summit just before sunset for good views. Note that vehicle entry and exit points are separate, and the path lacks lighting, so it’s best to leave before dark.

The bad part:
At entrance, a guide approached us insistently & in an aggressive manner, since we declined his services and he argued questioning why we’d pay a taxi but not him, this was unnecessary and left a poor impression.
In my opinion, you won't really need a guide from here, since it's pretty straightforward with clear directions & you can take your own sweet time to enjoy, OR you may choose to get one sit on your head and push you constantly throughout, upto you. You're already paying much for what it's worth.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: F SB
text: The view from Sigiriya Rock is without a doubt unique and very impressive. We were also lucky that it wasn't really busy, so the ascent and descent went smoothly in 30 to 40 minutes each without any backlog. However, the prices for tourists here (more than a hundred times higher than the prices for locals) are just outrageous. You can't see where all the money goes, and some of the steps don't look the most stable. Therefore, it's only half-recommended.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Atul Vispute
text: Good track with excellent view from top. But overpriced for foreigners US$ 40 per person ticket.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Toni-é MANI
text: Nice place with interesting history but the ticket price is crazy, 36 usd while locals pay only 30 cents. Even our guide was ashamed for that. Nothing justify this price, it got lack of infrastructure inside the park for elderly people to climb the stairs and also to make it more interesting in generally.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Dayal
text: The museum tells the history. The Mirror wall looks amazing though quite small room for visitors. The toilet under the foot charged 50 ruppe for local, for foreign tourists 7 times more. Crazy, we have already paid 36 dollars for the ticket.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Adhithyan Sundar
text:
Looks fascinating in the photos.
But once you climb the top, you can get a nice view of the surroundings greeneries only. There's nothing much to see at the top. The ruins are not that great.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Sherwin Chan
text:
Amazing views on the top!
However neither the conservation effort nor the tourist infrastructures on site seem to justify the high entrance price, especially adjusted to local terms
Of course first time traveler will still pay for the extortionate entrance fee, but this practice may stop people from making repeated visit to beautiful Sri Lanka

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Katherine Stevens
text: Pretty but very crowded. If you burn, make sure to bring sunscreen and expect to take a few hours because of the crowds and narrow stairway.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Sapan Patel
text: Excellent views and ancient architecture. Lots of steps, no disabled access. Limited bathroom facilities once on the rock, so recommended to go before. Great place to pick up some cheap souvenirs and are open to haggling. Disappointed by the steep pricing increase for foreign tourists, highly inflated at $30 but slightly cheaper when paying in rupees.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Michel
text: Interesting ruins and not too bad of a view of the surrounding landscape. However, it is totally overpriced (35 dollars?!) and run down with stray dogs, rabid squirrels and macaques. People really need to STOP feeding the animals. They need to fix that and lower the price. Watch out for the stray guides as well.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Samsul Arefin Sakib
text: Nature is not bad but for foreigners it's expensive.. Like if want to go up the lion rock you have to pay 35$.. If you are from SARC you have to pay 20$.. But for sri lankan it's 120₹ rupee..

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Katalin Farago
text: It's a historical place. Take care with monkeys, they an open your bag, and are aggressive! The stairs are OK

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Michael Bouda
text:
Yes, this is important place for the Sri Lanka history - but to be honest from a tourist perspective it is nothing extraordinary...

Allocate 2h If you planning to climb up.

You have to fork 5,400 rp to have access to the location. All you get is few ruins and rock you can climb on.

The rock itself is quite spectacular hence why I would recommend to skip it (as you can’t see it from the rock itself) and climb the Pidurangala - hill next to Sigiriya.

For 500Rp you will get
- Access to the temple at the bottom
- Beautiful view of Sigiriya

The climb is okay - I did it in thongs without any problems. You are not walking on lava field but stable, not sharp big rocks so you could do the final part barefoot...

There is a big rock on the top that provides a little shade. Great place for luch or picnic with the view!

A lot of people suggested sunrise/ sunset climb - which I have not done but understand the beauty of it so If you could, do it!!!

Up/down - about 25min

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: John
text: The lion paws make for a good photo, the views from the top are quite nice if it's not foggy but that's it. The site is poorly maintained and you can clearly see the damage done by tourists. The entrance fee is overpriced. Not a must visit in my opinion.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: JR Suarez
text:
The ticket office is in the same building as the archeological museum. The rock is beautiful place but with a very expensive entrance fee. They seemed to be obsessed with dollars as they only show the price in that currency in the ticket office. Worst of all they dont take cards...it is ridiculous for such a high price.
Is full of monkeys and unlike other places in Asia they are not aggressive or try to steal anything from you. They are very beautiful and you can see more than one type.
Is full of warning signs advising to keep quiet to avoid wasps attacks. I read a sign warning that if there is an attack they close the entry to the final part of the rock, from the lion’s paws upwards and there is no refund.
Be aware that you can start the visit with a blue sky and two hours later it can rain cats and dogs so dont forget to be prepared with water and for the rain.
No concessions for students.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Mia Barry
text: My husband and I climbed Sigiriya Rock at 4pm in the afternoon.  This was a good time because it was not as hot and there were less tourists .  We climbed it in about 40mins, note there is an entrance fee of 30usd each .  The rock has lots of old ruins and it used to be a fort for the king, he kicked Buddhist monks off the rock to build a fortress and the monks went to Pidurangala Rock (which we climbed for sunrise the next day) I preferred Pidurangala Rock much more and would recommend this over Sigiriya

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Tam GB
text:
Overpriced world heritage site. I understand the money is used to keep the sites but it feels too much for what it offers.

You can pay by card in usd and buy tickets online in advance too.

I was initially going to skip it due to pricing but people kept telling me it was worth it amd as one never knows if you'll be back to the country I thought I'd spend the cash and visit it.

It is a nice historic site and has some beautiful views from the top but for me it was not worth the price (half the price would have seem reasonable but 30usd is too much). If you're not a history geek and want some nice views Pidurungala offers beautiful views at a fraction of the price (+-3usd Vs 30usd).

It's a lot of stairs to get up the top so if you're unfit or have some mobility issues it's something to keep in mind.

Better visit early in the day, as soon as they open, so it's not so crowded and not too hot. Take some water and snacks with you.
Going up and down, checking everything out and stopping for pictures and videos it should take you a minimum of 2-3h to see.

The museum (where you buy the tickets in person) is not really worth it. But you can at least cool down a bit inside for 30min and see it after being outside.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Sneh K
text: Views weren't spectacular and it was a long queue in hot weather. 6000lkr for SARC countries, 11000lkr for other foreigners, place is just not worth the price.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Sanjay Tamil
text: 👋 hello . This is sigiriya hill . Natural mountain, only can view this nature . History said that one sinhala king make this place . But when he comes before that hill was made by Tamil king Ravanan this is the true . But this is a good tourist place local have to pay 100 forign people have to pay 30$ . It's to hard to climb if you are not fit at body don't try to climb 😉

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Tina Boudreault
text:
This is a wonderful site !
However price is way too expensive at 9350.
A lot of people decide to only hike the mountain beside it because it is too expensive.
Ticket need to be bought at museum or online.
Open around 6:30.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Charlotte Jetté Lemay
text:
Overpriced but nice activity to do for tourist. If you are on a budget, you can see the rock for free from the near by view point. We still decided to do it, and I'm satisfied but it's too pricey.

The organisation of the place isn't quite good. If you arrive from Dambulla by bus, the walk to the main entrance is 1,5km. After, you have to by your ticket (cash only, but there are ATMs on site) at the museum. Get your water and go to the toilet before waking up. There isn't any in the way.

The walk up seems kinda intense/impossible at first but it's well divided in many area to rest. Local people go it in flip flops but I would recommend doing it with proper athletic shoes and clothing.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: David C
text:
++Nice views
-- Too expensive and crowded (from our group of six people only two of us went up, the others stayed due to the claustrophobic feeling of an endless line) No reimbursement was done (30usd per person).
As many people advised, we should have gone to the mountain in front, much less crowded and cheaper

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Sujitha Ramanathan
text:
Lion rock is a small beautiful rock.

When you take photos of art don’t use flash. It’s prohibited there.

Preferable to climb at morning time (6am - 8 am)

And also be careful with bees.

Meantime near the Sigriya rock there is an another rock called pidurangala. It’s different and interesting.

For more information check pidurangala rock review.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Reggie Hurt
text: The line queues are long which makes the climb time long if it’s crowded. I skipped this 5,500 SriLankan Rupes rock climb  for the 500 SriLankan Rupee Pudurangala Cave Rock Temple Sigiriya instead.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Tharushi Gallage
text: Sigiriya is named one of the eight wonders of the world and rightfully so. The irrigation systems among other things are beyond impressive for its time. However, the climb is quite tiring. I wouldn’t advise those with weak health to make this climb. There are two separate entrances, one for locals and another for foreigners. The path is comparatively easier for the second entrance but the ticket price is relatively higher.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: I'm just vidu
text: I'm not a foreigner. But I went there with a foreigner. For foreigners the entrance fee is really higher than locals. Sigiriya is all marvellous but you have to pay to see this ancient place. After all it's understandable because this should be maintained well and protected.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Uthum De Saram
text: Best time to visit and enjoy is early morning cuz this place easily get crowded . For foreigners I suggest a to cm with a guide to get the best experience.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Mustafa K.
text: It is so expensive it is about 36usd. I recommend you the go near by Rock so you can take a photo from there. You pay a fee to pass by some temple i believe it was 1000 and it is way better view from there

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Albert Carbonell
text:
A must if you visit Sri Lanka!
The 30$ fee is a total rip off, just pay it though, but is a rip off :(

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: MUHAMMAD ALI HARIS
text: There is no need to stay Sigiriya. This is not worth it place. Stay somewhere else city form there come early morning visit Rock and go back. There is nothing in Sigiriya, only Rock mountains.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: андрей соколов
text: Museum is boring and awful. View from the rock is good, but it doesn't cost 30$. Temple near Kandy is higher, cheaper and more interesting

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Karthik Subramanian
text: The place itself is amazing but we reached on a day that was extremely Crowded and no one around to manage the crowds.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Dhasanthan
text: Top view is awesome just a historical place nothing special and the maintenance is very poor ..

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: mostafa amer
text: Worth visiting, you climb up (stairs) in not more than 30 mins. Great for photoshooting

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Tom Uijtdehaag
text: Nice place to visit, however the queues for both tickets and the stairs up are insane. Also the ticket price of 30 USD per person is too high for the experience.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Tomáš Teslík
text:
The first disappointment was the entrance fee. Locals pay 120 rupees, while tourists are charged over 10,500 rupees! This is more than the entrance fees for some major European tourist attractions! I understand a discount for locals, but such a vast difference is pure exploitation of tourists.

Once again, you'll be approached by guides here who want, guess what? Of course, to be paid. The local shabby canteen offers just three types of flatbreads and nothing else.

The area beyond the ticket check is indeed nice, though for 10,500 rupees, I expected more. Narrow stairs are crowded with tourists who have no idea where to go. There's a hint of two lanes (one going up and one coming down), but no one follows it, and there's no effective navigation system. The staff, who are presumably supposed to guide visitors, sit with their feet up and phones in hand.

All these local shortcomings are overshadowed by the grandeur of the place, which is truly magnificent and definitely worth a visit. It's one of the most beautiful sites I've seen in Sri Lanka so far.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Hugo Schurink
text: Great to climb, cool staircases.  But in hindsight I would have climbed the mountain opposite of Sigiriya. Same sight, lower price

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Hugo
text: Great experience but the tourist entry ticket is waaaay to expensive (30$ is too much)

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Rocky Crag
text:
Lions rock has a beautiful view at sunset, sunrise is not so good as the region is normally cloudy early morning. The view from Pidirangala ie P Rock is very similar to Lion Rock but Lion rock costs about£30 to enter and P rock around £3. Lion rock is no eighth wonder of the world, its nice enough but over priced for what it is.
The surrounding town is shacks of cafes and restaurants in the forests. There is a lovely walk around P rock and lion rock bases that can be finished off with an ascent of P rock.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Mathis Heltoft
text: Lions Rock is an incredible natural phenomen and has significant Sri Lankian historical value. The climb and view is stunning and beautiful. Now the rating is due to the extreme overprice of 30 USD per person and the fact they don't even accept credit card. Off course we do pay when we are there in SL, but there is absolutely no reason for this price. Pure money machine. Locals pay less than 50 dollar cent (which is fair, but no reason for tourist exploitation). If you are on budget go to Pido mountain, which is just as good.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Tharindu Wickramasekara
text:
Sigiriya: Overrated for Foreign Visitors

Sigiriya is undeniably a site of immense historical value and a marvel to behold, but if you’re a foreigner, I can’t recommend visiting. Here’s why:

First off, the entry fee for foreigners is $35 (even if you are a resident), while locals pay just $0.50. It’s not about the price, but about what you actually get for it. Despite paying that much, you’re still stuck in the same long queues as everyone else, with no added perks or special treatment.

Once inside, you’re swarmed by tourist guides who are aggressively trying to squeeze more money out of you. And here’s the kicker—there isn’t even a single toilet available once you enter the main area! After all that, you’re not allowed to take photos of the famous frescos, even without flash, which feels unnecessarily restrictive.

To top it off, if you happen to come down after sunset, there are no lights to guide your way, which can be a bit risky.

Instead, I’d suggest trying **Pidurangala**. For just $3, you can enjoy equally stunning views, a peaceful atmosphere, and a much more budget-friendly experience. Sigiriya might be historic, but Pidurangala feels like the better deal for travelers looking for beauty without the hassle.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Christoph Müller
text: Impressive rock with old ruines, but too many people, go up before 8am

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Fazeem Ahamed
text: Ancient  place . one of the  proud of srilankan  culture.  it is built by king kasiyappa

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Rob
text: Beautiful, interesting, and nice; but I'm not sure the price point of $36 (approx. 12,000 rupees) is really worth it. I think they've overvalued the attraction.  I met many people in Sri Lanka who didn't even bother going to this, especially families, and they just went to the other rock instead which is $3 (1,000 rupees).  They've overpriced this place by $14-16. I'd say low $20s is reasonable.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Aarti Shamdasani
text: Stairs are steep. Some parts no railing. Walking back down is more challenging. Took 1 hour to go up and 1 hour down. View is good , was a sunny day. Try to go early before sun is strong. Unfortunately it was past noonish and sun was strong. However many stops to rest on the way up. Wear sunblock and cap, walking shoes. Take water. Shops selling drinks  souvenirs etc are charging premium prices. Avoid the guides trying to chat you up and assist you if you dont require any help to climb. Asking for ridiculous money. We got scammed somewhat. The ticket price for locals and foreigners is different. Non national sri lankan residents can pay local price bring your proof of id,passport, residence visa proof etc otherwise you have to pay foreigner rate which is absurd. The tuks also ask for crazy rates to drive up to the entrance from the car park, try to bargain.  The place should be more well maintained considering the premium rates foreigners have to pay. The water fountain at the entrance of the rock not functioning . They should have more railings to hold onto going up and down or make the steps more even.  Not suitable for elderly or small kids or people who need assistance. Good luck!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: saurabh anand
text: Overhyped place. Not worth, maybe early morning for the sunrise you can. But else can be avoided.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Rashmi J
text:
Great place and views but 10000 LKR for tourists is costlier than our hotel stay which is 90% more than what locals pay. It's ridiculous. Is it worth it? Yes, if you are into history and willing to pay some more for a guide otherwise no.
If you're from SAARC, take your passport with you. They don't accept digital copies.

Overall, Sigiriya left me feeling exploited but Kandy-Ella were awesome!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Aravindan Srinivasan
text:
Ancient rock, people who are interested in trucking can visit this place. The entrance ticket is almost 5500 LKR /person for foreigners, which is very expensive to visit this place.

Those who think can't climb 1200 steps please don't buy the tickets, then it will be a waste of money.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Kuba Porycki
text: Mixed feelings. It is impressive when you’re looking from the ground. Once you get there, it’s just a brunch of bricks.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Sasha D'Amours
text: Nice city but too much touristic. The price are expensive for Sri lanka but its worth visiting for the hike and the organic vegqn cooking class! We saw elephant on the road. No need to pay for a safari!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: RUTH Kirby-Smith
text: Well worth a visit and the effort of climbing but start early as it gets crowded.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Melanie Forbes
text: We opted to not go in to the monument due to the very high entrance price. But, we found that walking around the base was beautiful and clean.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: J.R.C Lambert
text:
It will be a adventurous memory on climbing sigiriya,and nothing better at the top,just the rests of the castles .You can get a free aqua treatment up there at the pond.And at the way of returning you can see the paintings and the mirror wall.
But it was a good place for visit once.But you should spend 3 hours+ there for climbing up and down.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: dev d
text: 3 stars only because of exorbitant rates of tickets for foreigners. Everything else about this place is an unmissable experience.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Monica Gentile
text: It is a very interesting place but pls avoid local holidays: we went on a Sunday morning and it was so full with both tourists and locals that we had to proceed in long (and very noisy) que all the way up. that spoiled the visit quite a bit. In my opinion the best way to experience it is actually from a distance, though the visit could be worth if they cut the exorbitant 36 USD fee: I agree to pay an entrance but that is way too high in Sri Lanka.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Léo H.
text: The lions rock itself is pretty impressive. The entrance fee is expensive though. We paid 30 USD. With that being said: It looks way better from below than when you are actually on it. You should consider just staying down. If you want a good view, climb the Pidurangala. It’s way cheaper and the hike is also much nicer.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: curious110
text: Very expensive! The facilities don't look very inviting. For a world heritage this place lacks a specific standard which I expected. Toilets where smelly and dirty. Stairways to the rock didn't look trustworthy. Also there are people who want to make you buy a tour or souvenirs and stuff, whom are very annoying knowing that you frkn pay 30 $ for climbing that rock. That's enormous!  I guess UNESCO should check more often, if this place is worth being called world heritage, to be honest. If I pay that much I don't want to be bothered.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Kate C
text:
NO CARDS, CASH ONLY!
The price difference for tickets for locals and foreigners is just crazy (over x100). For 30$ per person there is hardly any infrastructure (no drinking water, no audio guides etc). The climb was shorter than I expected, it took us 2 hours and a half to cover the whole area. The place itself is beautiful!

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Fry
text:
30USD to enter is a joke. Another place the 🇱🇰 government imposes a crazy price from the locals point of view to tourists. At least, this place is well maintained in contrast to the national parks.

I would advise you to skip going there and save your money.

The view is cool but not worth the effort, neither the money.

Alternatively, my advice is for you to go to the other rock on the north, it's cheaper (LKR 1,000), more fun to walk through and the view is really rewarding since you can see the Lion rock from there.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

#visit#srilanka

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

The climb is not extremely tough but challenging enough, bring plenty of water and be ready to re-apply sunscreen at the top as you'll sweat most of it off and there is very little shade.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

The infrastructure to climb is deteriorated and insecure, in some parts the steel railings are corroded by humidity and replaced only by ropes, the place is beautiful but dangerous, any trip can cause an accident or kill you.
The price is too expensive US$30 per person.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

It's a very interesting sight but on our 5 day trip to Sri Lanka we definitely thought we could of passed on it.
It's expensive for what it is and very hot there. So unless your really into it , seeing pictures is more than enough.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

Only did the basic minimum in explaining and hurried us to return down from the top of the rock so he can be finished his job earlier. description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Prianka Nalin Seneviratne
text: Way too crowded and way too expensive for foreigners. People movement at the top should be better managed to avoid damage and public safety. Have a online scheduling and ticketing  system to help people plan their time. Have knowledgeable professional guides and with fixed rates.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Russ Conway
text: I have just returned from the Lion Rock, so here's a review while it is still fresh in my head. First of all, the site in magnificent. However, is a $30 entrance fee really necessary? It didn't feel like good value to me. Next up, there are an army of "guides" who will appraoch you for work (inside and outside of the park), if you don't have a "guide" already with you - This becomes irratating. If the park's management wants to charge $30 entrance fee, why not include a guide within the price of the ticket and put back some money into the local economy. Then clean out all the grifters who try to befriend you along the way. Coming out of the park, you are met by a colony of sellers - it's like going through a major airport's duty free. It's a beautiful place but it could be a lot better organised and managed.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Yossi Mesika
text: I actually haven't climbed the rock itself as the entrance fee seemed to high for me. Walking around it with the water canals is really nice walk. I found the nearby rock that offers similar views and cheaper for budget travellers. You can walk here from the town as it's very close.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Iromi Nawarathna
text: Amazing  360 view from the summit. Worth watching ancient arts. Have to be careful with steel stair case which has gaps between steps.Hence rainy days cannot be recommended. Restaurants near by need improvements regards to food quality,test and sanitary facilities.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Christina Kiegel
text:
I thought the views were great until I climbed the smaller rock. I also don't understand why the price is so high for tickets.

The ticket office closes at 5pm! The rock is open until 7pm, but arriving at the ticket office at 5:05 means you have to come back the next day..

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Nathan Dodds
text: Best aattraction in Sri Lanka. My advice get there early to beat the crowds and the midday heat. Its worth paying the 1500LKR for a local guide to take you a third of the way up and beat the lines of people at the bottom. From there the staircase gets very narrow but at least the line moves. The only annoyance is some people, generally other tourists, pushing in. I saw some people struggle with the steepness of the steps and the heat of the sun with little shade so make sure you take plenty of water, a hat and some sunscreen. You wont get opportunities to buy water until you get to the exit carpark and if you run out of water by the time you get too the top its a long slow climb back to the bottom and this is where I saw most people struggle. The views from the top are amazing and on a clear day you can see for miles. You can easily fill a whole day here if you take your time and take it all in. Take plenty of cash there are some great market stalls in the exit carpark with heaps of great souvenier opportunities. I mark the whole experience down to 3 stars for the initial entry fee which is $30USD. Very expensive for the amount of time you spend standing in lines. Only worth it if you really want too appreciate the views from the top as the walk up and down is really quite unpleasant. If you want a free experience the views of the rock at the bottom are quite spectacular and well worth the experience on its own.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Rupali Shinde
text: If you are on a budget trip do not visit this place view is okay. Architecture is good. But this is very crowdy place and there is only traces of fort on the rock no physical fort present. So watch your time and money.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

You need to climb alot of stairs to reach at the top. avoid visiting during noon , try to visit in morning. carry water bottle & umbrella, apply sunscreen
The view from top is cool but dont expect too much.

overall it was a good experience. the feeling you get when you reach at the top after fighting heat is fabulous.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Max M
text: Now I'm not saying its not impressive its just a shame how much you feel like you are being sent through the tourist trap. Its also very scary if your scared of heights (I'm not but my legs went on the overhanging spiral staircase). I recommend getting there before 8am to beat the crowds and the heat! My top tip would be to visit the next door rock (Pidurangala Rock) which is free and offers spectacular views of Lion Rock.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Bali Bock
text: This place could have been such a nice experience, had it not been for the irresponsible staff and security.  For over 5,000 rupees you are paying to walk thru an ancient site that seems decently setup for visitors but the procedures for there security and staff are hardly professional or even moral. There are stray dogs running everywhere as well as dogs with collars who obviously reside in the park, and they frequently fight with the stray dogs inside the park which seems a terrible safety concerns given all the people who come there. The stairs and railing leading up the top site are rusted and exposed, for the price of admission I would expect some decent maintenance on the most important safety issue in the whole park, the stairs! My friends and I went for the afternoon and watched sunset which was wonderful but then noticed all the staff had left the park,  no lights anywhere to indicate exits, and we stumbled through the dark getting lost by going through the wrong exit to get to our vehicle. When we finally found an exit with security staff they were not helpful or friendly but instead asked us to show them our tickets as if we had some how managed to sneak into the park without paying. After showing our tickets we proceeded to walk back to the main entrance but were questioned about where we were going to which we replied that we have vehicles in the main entrance area. There response was anything but helpful and did not even offer to assist us in returning to our vehicle in the dark(7:00pm). For the money they charge to foreigners for entrance I would hope to at least see some decent protocols like have a guard do a closing sweep of the park to check for guest and some decent help to those who do stay late and get lost. This place seems like all they care about is your money and then you’re on your own. That is funny and almost ironic given the amount of signs posted stating not to walk on ancient stones or the grass, or how illegal it is to deface the ruins, all of which could have occurred because no one checks the park after 5pm. The amount of false “guides” in the park who are trying to hustle tourists was also quite bothersome, not a pleasant way to see a world heritage such and certainly not what I would expect from a place that is supposedly the pride of Sri Lanka.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: J
text: Went all the way to the top in the pouring rain late in the afternoon and only made it back just before closing time. Not sure whether it's actually worth it considering it's $50 AUD (tourist price)! That's extremely pricy even in our currency. It was so steep and slippery couldn't see anything at the top due to the fog. I couldn't imagine young children being able to do this. I would just visit a temple with a view. You can't take photos of any of the artifacts. You would only go there just to climb this rock.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Josten Ma
text:
It was a pleasant and impressive place to visit in Sri Lanka and would recommend this to everyone.  The reason, I did not rate this high is because of our bad experience with the local guide.
After paying for entrance fee, we were given an English speaking guide.
Everything went well except at the end of the tour he started pressuring us for tips which we were gladly prepare to do.  But then when we were getting the money ready to give him, his smiling face turned sour and was being really rude and nasty. On reflection, we thought may be when he saw the first small bill in our stack of money.  It has been my habit traveling overseas, to put small bills first of my cash.
After we got back to our car and was talking to our driver, he mentioned that he paid the guide at a wages that is high comparing to average income of locals.
On one hand, tourism helps the local economy and it is a good thing.  But it is sad to see greed take over in some cases.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.

eh, what was once a medieval fortress now a tourist trap.

It's a nice place to visit, the view from the top is lovely. Also there's an interesting back story that the guides will tell you. It can get crowded sometimes though.

The tickets are rather pricey, but again if you're visiting Sri Lanka this is one of the more interesting places to visit. Be sure to go early in the morning or in the evening when the heat ain't too bad.

On your way to the top (if you're a tourist) you will be harassed multiple times by people trying to sell you stuff or someone insisting that they be your guide. that's annoying.

Well, it would have been a wonderful place hundreds of years ago but now the surrounding area has just been destroyed by human activities, commercial development and littering.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Mr. Rimas
text:
Amazing place.

Title: Majestic Sigiriya Rock: A Mesmerizing Journey into Ancient Sri Lankan History

Sigiriya Rock stands tall as a testament to Sri Lanka's rich cultural heritage and architectural brilliance. Located in the heart of the island nation, this ancient rock fortress has captivated visitors from around the world with its sheer grandeur and historical significance.

Ascending the rock, one is immediately struck by its imposing presence, rising 200 meters above the surrounding lush greenery. The journey to the top is an adventure in itself, with narrow staircases, ancient frescoes, and the famous Lion's Paw Terrace, which guards the entrance to the summit.

Upon reaching the summit, visitors are rewarded with breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding countryside. The remnants of King Kashyapa's palace, built in the 5th century, are awe-inspiring. The meticulous planning and engineering that went into constructing this fortress are nothing short of remarkable.

The frescoes adorning the walls of Sigiriya are a highlight not to be missed. These vibrant paintings, known as the Sigiriya Damsels, depict celestial nymphs and courtly figures. Despite the passage of centuries, the colors have retained their vibrancy, leaving visitors in awe of the artistic talent of ancient Sri Lankan craftsmen.

Sigiriya's remarkable water gardens are another marvel of ancient engineering. The symmetrical layout of fountains, ponds, and water channels showcases the sophistication of the hydraulic systems that were ahead of their time. Walking through these meticulously designed gardens is a tranquil experience, transporting visitors to a bygone era.

The historical and cultural significance of Sigiriya cannot be overstated. Recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, it serves as a window into Sri Lanka's glorious past. It is a testament to the island's ancient civilization and its contribution to art, architecture, and urban planning.

In conclusion, Sigiriya Rock is an extraordinary destination that leaves a lasting impression on all who visit. Its historical significance, architectural marvels, and stunning views make it a must-visit for anyone seeking to explore the wonders of ancient Sri Lanka. Prepare to be enchanted by this majestic rock fortress, for it truly is a treasure to behold.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya

text: Went first day at 8am! Big mistake, the crowd was too much. We stood for 1 hour is same position on staircase to climb. We went down and came back next day at 6:30am to wait for 7am ticket booth to open. For 20+ minutes we were only people at the top, amazing. GO EARLY

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Catherine C.
text:
I probably expected too much of this place. After Angkok Wat and Borobodur, I found Sigiriya not as interesting visually. Good photos are not possible when you're inside the fortress, unless you have a drone. You have to go to Pidurangala Rock instead.

That said, it was still interesting to see the ruins. It's worth it to hire a guide, so you'll learn more about the place. Otherwise it is just another pile of rubble. It is also a good idea to visit the Museum before you go to the fortress, as it gives you some background about the fortress.

Last but not least, the climb up is not difficult (even steps and metal staircases on well-marked paths). If you start from the foreign visitor carpark (instead of the local car park), it is also not strenuous. It's about 45 mins from bottom up. I suffer from vertigo but at no point did I feel frightened on the way up.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Aseem
text:
Sigiriya Lion Rock is an ancient rock fortress known for its massive column of rock that reaches nearly 200 meters high. The site dates back to the reign of King Kasyapa (477-495 AD), who chose this site as his new capital. He decorated the walls with frescoes, and built an impressive palace right on top of the rock column, accessible only through the mouth of an enormous carved lion.

The ruins of the capital built by the parricidal King Kassapa (477–95) lie on the steep slopes and at the summit of a granite peak standing some 180m high (the ‘Lion’s Rock’, which dominates the jungle from all sides). A series of galleries and staircases emerging from the mouth of a gigantic lion constructed of bricks and plaster provide access to the site.

Sigiriya is one of Sri Lanka’s most famous historical monuments. Residents call this ancient palace and fortress complex the eighth wonder of the world and it has significant archeology and attracts thousands of tourists annually. This is probably the most visited tourist destination in Sri Lanka.

The Sigiriya rocky plain, made of magma from an extinct volcano, is 200 meters higher than the surrounding forest. The exhibition surprises visitors with its unique harmony in nature and human imagination.

Thanks to this lion, the palace got Sigiriya. The word Sigiriya is derived from the word Sihagri, meaning Lion Rock. The western walls of Sigiriya are almost exclusively covered with frescoes, made during the Kasyapa period. Eighteen frescoes are still alive today.

The fresco depicts a naked woman and is seen as a picture of Kasyapa’s wife and concubine, or as a priest containing religious rites. Although the identity of the unknown woman is depicted in the mural, this unique ancient painting celebrates the beauty of women and has a remarkable historical impact.

One of the best features of Sigiriya is its mirrored walls. In the old days it was so refined that the king could see the reflection. The mirror walls are painted with inscriptions and poems written for Sigiriya’s guests.

The oldest inscription is from the ninth century. The inscription says that Sigiriya was a tourist destination more than a thousand years ago. Today it is strictly forbidden to paint walls.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: anatole opticien
text:
5200 rupees and no explication boards, nothing to explain you what was there and what for. And it’s not clean you can find rubbish everywhere on the ground on top of the rock.

30USD per pax for a tourist site it’s even more expensive than in Europe we do expect something cleaned up during the day and some explanations signs.

Respect your own heritage guys. It is not just a business.

Although the view and ruines are amasing definitely worth it ! Try the sunset there ! You will have a good time on the rock you can easily spend 3/4h

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Prasun Mondal
text:
Overall place is very good.
Main problem is that there is no light in the area , on the site or stairs and it gets pretty dark after evening. We entered the site at 5pm. When we reached the top it was 6pm. On our way back it was pitch black dark. No lights on the way out through the jungle and stairs.we were saved by the mobile leds. Administration should look into this and put lights for proper illumination or they should put indications or signs so that people may become aware of the situation.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: manish pandey
text: Pathetic crowd management, the authorities charge $30 and they cant even construct two separate pathways for going up and down. At the top of the hill, after wasting 3 hours in single file  queues , there is not much to see except for ruins and boulders.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: René B.
text:
Nice place if you like temples and ancient stuff.

30$ are still a bit expensive though.
There is no drinking water or something on the way up.
It’s very crowded. And rude locals are squeezing past all the time, although there is no space - that’s very annoying.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Pragati
text:
It is a full day trip and you need to carry your water bottle with you entry fee charged is too much from foreigners
Difficult to go with children not recommended in rainy days also in sunny days so better start your trip in the morning itself and and end it before afternoon

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Noswad
text: Expensive 30 dollars the entrance, too much tourists place

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Laetitia Lake
text: It was a wonderful experience but not sure it was worth the 4,900lkr ($30USD) to walk up to the top of the rock. There is a smaller rock next to it which is easier to climb and only 500lkr to walk up. Not many people know about this one and you can see just as beautiful views. The walk up to the top of Sirigiya was very busy and touristy so best to walk up towards the end of the day, you also get to watch the sunset. You don't need a guide so don't get conned into paying for one at the bottom. There were three puppies at the top with their mother which were no doubt born up there, I wish someone would carry them down as there is no food and harsh living conditions at the top of the rock. Instead everyone stood and took pictures of them! What a world we live in.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Chandani Jayasekara
text: Apart from the view and all we had to wait approximately 5 hours to get to the top and make the way back. It's great up there but still that queue is pretty bad.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Andrew McKinnon
text: Okay but much more expensive than the other rock 1km away and the views from the top aren’t as good. The historical sights and the museum were interesting which the other place had less of.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya

text: To be honest 30$ for ticket is to much for this place. There are a lot of people moving very slowly on the stairs. The ruins are not so beutyful if you saw Palanaruwa before. The pictures of naked women you can see very fast becouse of people behind sturt to push after momebt you stay to see it more then one second, and once you pass them you can't come back. The small rock near is more intetresting and cheaper))

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya

text:
Awesome place, I liked it a lot. BUT - people are totally getting ripped off. They charge 30 US Dollars to let you climb that rock. Children pay half, but children only count from ages 6-12. A 13 year old kid has to pay 30$ as well.
I've seen older/obese people crawl up there.. so be fit or maybe do something else.
Also you should go there early in the morning  (opens at 7am) when it's not that hot. Wear sun cream.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
title: Sigiriya
name: Chris K
text: 30 dollars per person is a typical rip-off done in Sri Lanka. The neighbour rock climb is only 4 dollars and is open early morning so you can be on top for sunrise. This one does not open so early. We did not do it as it's about the sunrise view ... your choice to do both. If you do this one, do it rather in the late afternoon due to heat.

description: The ruins of a 5th-century city, anchored by a towering rock fortress accessible by steep stairs.
